Established as she was in a state of perfect equa­ nimity, it seemed that there was nothing which could shake The Sword Of Truth 157 the initiative and efficiency of the Mother. The marriage cere­ mony went off successfully despite the fact that Sugunanan­ dan stood on the side lines casually observing the whole event. The Holy Mother's words, “Today’s enemy is tomorrow’s friend,” came true and the same was repeated for the other daughters’ marriages as well. There is a saying in Malayalam, ‘The jasmine which grows in the front of the house has no fragrance.’ The meaning of this is that if a person becomes great and famous, he will never be recognized by his or her own community. Many are the right­ eous souls who have suffered the truth of the adage. The Holy Mother would say, '‘Suppose somebody was listening to a beautiful song on the radio. He was really enjoying the sweet­ ness of the song's melody when his close friend came into the room and said, ‘Do you know who is singing that song? It is our neighbour, Shankar.’ Immediately, the one who was en­ joying the song turned off the radio saying, ‘Oh, what kind of a singer is he? It’s terrible!’ Children, this is the attitude of the people. It is difficult for them to accept a person who they have always known and moved closely with.” Thus it was in the case of the Holy Mother. The circumstances that surrounded the Mother were far from kind. This young fisherfolk girl was supported by none. As the devotees were from different parts of the country, they could do nothing against the ignorant and uncultured villag­ ers who tormented the Holy Mother. Besides that, the devo­ tees for the most part believed that the Mother was possessed by Krishna and Devi during the Bhava Darshan. They failed to understand the depth or fullness of the Mother’s God-Reali­ sation. Not only that; most of the devotees in those early days came primarily for worldly achievements, not spiritual upliftment. If their desire was fulfilled, they would return only when another desire cropped up. If their desire didn’t get fulfilled, then they would never come again and their devo­ tion to the Mother ended there.
94: The Mother had neither an inch of her own land nor a penny at her disposal. Her own kith 158 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI and kin were non co-operative and deadly opposed her. Her own family members were against her wish and will. They nei­ ther helped nor encouraged her in any way. On one occasion, a devotee asked the Mother about the immensity of the trials and tribulations which she had to con­ front both during and after her sadhana days. They wondered how their own Realization could ever take place if they had to undergo so much suffering which they doubted they could forbear. Mother was quick to point out that her own life only showed that it was possible to realize God even under the worst possible circumstances. It will be of great interest to the readers to know how the Holy Mother raised an Ashram from the midst of this raging storm and this will be related in the next chapter. * * * * CHAPTER TEN THE MOTHER OF IMMORTAL BLISS Always understand that Mother is omnipres­ ent. Have the faith that Mother's Self and your Self are one. Children, the mother who gave birth to you may look after matters relating to this life; nowadays even this is very rare. But Mother's aim is to lead you in such a way that you can enjoy bliss in all your future lives. -Mata Amritanandamayi-The Guru's Wisdom cannot be learned From even gods of the higher worlds; The Guru's Knowledge awakes in him Who serves his Guru with purest love. GURU GITA VERSE 22 A Band Of Youths "Children, the coolness of the breeze, the beams of the moon, the vastness of space and all things of the world-these are all permeated with Divine Consciousness. Knowing and experienc­ ing this Truth is the Goal of human birth. In this dark age a band of youths, renouncing all, will set out to disseminate spiritual light throughout the world." -MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI As early as 1976, a young boy of twenty named Unni Krish­ nan from the village of Alappad came to meet the Holy Mother. He was rather like a mendicant. Although he had a home and family, he only rarely visited there. After meeting the Holy Mother, he developed a strong thirst to lead a spin- 162 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI tual life. Understanding this, a year later, the Holy Mother entrusted him with the daily worship in the temple and al­ lowed him to stay in her presence. He spent his daytimes in­ side the small shrine doing the daily worship and reciting the Sri Lalita Sahasranamam1 which the Holy Mother had in­ structed him in. Following this, he would be engaged in other spiritual practices and the rest of his time was spent reading scriptural texts or writing devotional poetry’. In the night, he would sleep on the temple verandah and use only a thin towel spread on the floor as a mattress. He was so calm and quiet that
95: none among the visitors would understand that he was stay ing there. Thus, he became the first resident of the future Ashram. 1 Sacred mantra consisting of the thousand Names of the Divine Mother. 2 Situated 20 kilometres nonh of Vallickavu. 3 Now known as Amritatma Chaitanya after receiving traditional brahmacharya diksha from the Holy Mother. 4 Now known as Adhyatma Chaitanya.By the end of 1978, the nucleus for the Ashram grew when a group of well-educated young men, renouncing hearth and home, took refuge at the feet of the Holy Mother with the purpose of God-Realization and service of humanity as their one aim. Attracted by the magnetic personality and all-em­ bracing love of the Holy Mother, these young men were in­ spired to lead the life divine against all odds. Most of them hailed from the town of Haripad1 2 and came from upper class families. After meeting the Holy Mother, it became their strong conviction that following the path shown by the Mother was the ultimate goal that they could aspire to in their lives. Within the course of one month Sreekumar, Ramesh Rao, Venugopal, Harikumara Pai, Ramakrishnan, Balagopalan (Balu)3 and Chandru4 came to meet the Holy Mother and humbly requested her to guide them to their chosen goal. However, Sugunanandan still discouraged them from stay- . ng permanently near the Holy Mother. This was primarilyThe Mother Of Immortal Bliss 163 due to the fact that his other daughters remained unmarried. These young aspirants were either still in college or holding jobs, except for Balu who had just completed his college stud­ ies. They would come to see the Mother nearly every day or every other day while at the same time continuing to fulfill their responsibilities in the world. For the most part, their sudden transformation from worldly youth to seekers of God created havoc among their family and friends. In their minds, the Holy Mother was a witch who had hypnotised their sons through the use of her master sorcery. Ever ready to find fault with the Holy Mother, the rationalists took up the cause. They began feed­ ing the news media sensational stories in order to provoke public reaction against the Holy Mother. The devotees and young men became worried about the appearance of these fabricated stories in the newspaper. When the Mother came to know of their anxiety, she burst into laughter and said, “We are not those letters and words printed on a piece of paper. Continue your spiritual practices without wasting your time paying attention to such things. Those who oppose today will become devotees tomorrow." As the days passed, this declaration of the Holy Mother be­ came perfectly true. In November of that year, Chandru, a brahmana lad from Alleppey5 came to Idamannel to meet the Holy Mother. The first visit itself created a great transformation in him. He came to visit the Mother now and then whenever possible. He developed an intense desire to relinquish worldly life and sought the
96: advice of the Holy Mother as to where he could stay in order to do his spiritual practices as, in those days, it was Sugunanandan's habit to chase away any young aspirant who desired to stay in the presence of the Holy Mother. Chan­ dru also had to face Sugunanandan’s scoldings one evening and had been ordered to leave Idamannel. Greatly pained at heart, Chandru asked the Holy Mother to suggest a suitable 5 A coastal town 65 kilometres north of Vallickavu.The iMother Of Immortal Bliss 165 a cave on the sacred mountain Arunachala. The first two days he subsisted only on leaves and water. On the third day, in the evening, for lack of food he fainted calling aloud, ‘Amma!’ In a letter to the Mother he wrote, “It was about 5 o’clock in the afternoon when I fainted due to hunger. I was lying on the mountain in a semi-conscious state. Just then, I clearly heard Mother’s voice calling, ‘My son!’ I felt that my forehead was being gently rubbed by someone. When I opened my eyes I saw Mother standing before me in her white clothes. I was extremely thrilled at the sight!” After the Holy Mother received this letter, the devotees understood that it was exactly at that moment in Vallickavu when the Holy Mother had suddenly called out, “Oh my son!” and turned to a devotee sitting nearby saying, “Chandra in Tiruvannamalai has been starving since three days and now he is crying to see me!” After this incident, Chandru never had any difficulty to get food regularly. In the absence of a proper place to do his spiritual prac­ tices, Chandru would spend his days on the mountain and his nights sleeping at the foot of the hill. Coming down from the mountain, the first person that he met was an Australian woman named Gayatri. After a couple of days he met Mad- husudhana,10 a native of Reunion Island, whose descendents had come from India. All three felt a stream of love binding them together. Recalling the words of the Holy Mother, Chandru had a strong feeling that both of them were her chil­ dren. He began talking to them about the Holy Mother and showed them a small photo of her. Gayatri was enchanted by the blissful countenance and the glowing eyes of the Mother in the photo. 10 Now known as Prcmatma Chaitanya.Although Gayatri would regularly try to meditate, she was not satisfied with her spiritual progress. After seeing the Holy Mother’s photo and hearing about her selfless love and compassion, Gayatri had her first spiritual experience. To put it in her own words, “I saw a flash of light within me and dis-166 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI cerned the living form of the Mother in that light. Suddenly from within me arose the cry, 'Amma! Amma! Amma!' Then all thoughts subsided and my mind sank into stillness. When I opened my eyes and looked at the clock, I realised that twenty minutes had passed.
97: I had been unaware of anything. ” Madhu, eager to share the happiness which he experienced while hearing about the Holy Mother, introduced Chandru to an American devotee named Nealu who was contemplative by nature. His spiritual teacher, who was a direct disciple of Sri Ramana Maharshi, had passed away four years earlier. Serving his teacher, Nealu had been living in Tiruvannamalai for the past 11 years. At this time, he was mostly bedridden suffering from acute pain in the stomach and spine. He was hardly able to sit or walk even for a few moments. The doctors were at a loss to know the cause or cure. Coming to know of Chandru's difficulty to find a place for meditation, Nealu offered his late teacher's cottage for Chan­ dru's use. Chandru told him about the Holy Mother but Nc- alu was at first not very interested. He had already seen many great saints and was only concerned to cure his illness so that he could continue with his sadhana. With that idea in mind, he asked Chandru to take him to meet the Holy Mother after the completion of Chandru's vow of silence. Then Nealu gave the young sadhak a clock and rudrakshamala thinking that these items may be helpful in his spiritual practice. Recalling the Holy Mother's words that all would come unasked, Chandru felt overwhelmed with emotion and earnestly started his vow. One day while circumambulating Arunachala, Chandru noticed a tall white man chanting Tamil verses while moving with a group of people around the hill. It was the day of Sri Ramana's birthday celebrations. When Chandru glanced at the man, he also looked at Chandru, though with an air of pride. Chandru thought, “Though proud, he too seems to be the son of the Holy Mother.” This man was, in fact, a devotee from France named Ganga11 who later came to stay near the Holy Mother.The Mother Of Immortal Bliss 167 After forty one days of silence had been observed, Chan­ dru and Nealu journeyed to Vallickavu. Nealu’s first meeting with the Holy Mother was highly significant.12 As he himself describes, “The first four days I spent there, I felt as if I was in Heaven itself, such was the bliss experienced in the Mother’s Presence! One evening at the end of Devi Bhava, Mother was standing at the door of the temple and I was standing outside gazing at her with joined palms. I was overflowing with joy. At that moment, I saw her physical form disappear into an expanding radiance which spread all around and engulfed everything visible. That expansive light suddenly contracted into a blazing pinpoint of unbearably dazzling light and then I Now known as Sarvatma Chaitanya. II A detailed description can be found in Nealu’s book, “On The Road To Free­ dom" by Neal Rosner, Cassandra Press, Boulder, Colorado, U.S.A.I felt as if that light had entered into me. For three days I could get no sleep due to the spiritual intoxication of
98: that experi­ ence. After this, I could think only of the Holy Mother day and night. I decided to be near her until the end of my life in order to take guidance from her and sene her.” Nealu returned to Tiruvannamalai with Chandru to settle his affairs and came back to Vallickavu accompanied by Gayatri who was keenly desirous of serving the Holy Mother. Strangely enough, Sugunanandan welcomed all of them as if they were his own children. For the first time in three years, Nealu felt some relief from his sickness and could move about doing small works. When Nealu returned from Tiruvannamalai, he expressed his wish to the Holy Mother, “I do not want to go away from here. I want to stay with you forever as your humble servant.” The Mother told him, “Son, I don’t have an inch of land that is my own. Ask the father. Anyhow, we need a place to stay.” It was a great surprise to everyone when Sugunanandan agreed to donate a small piece of land on which was built a modest hut of woven coconut palm leaves. All told, it meas­ ured 9 by 18 feet. One comer was used as a kitchen for prepar­ ing drinks for the Holy Mother, but daily meals were still pre­168 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI pared in the main house. This hut served as shelter for the Holy Mother along with Nealu, Balu and Gayatri. This marked the informal beginning of the Ashram. After his first meeting with the Holy Mother, Balu left his home and spent most of his rime with the Mother. It was his good fortune to get the permission of Sugunanandan to re­ main at Idamannel on a permanent basis. Thus, when Nealu came from Tiruvannamalai to stay permanently, Balu also settled there. Having returned from Tiruvannamalai, Chandru was only able to stay with the Holy Mother for two months before she sent him to Sandheepany Sadhanalaya in Bombay to un­ dergo a three year course in Vedanta. After the arrival of Nealu and Gayatri, Ganga and Madhu also came and settled at the feet of the Holy Mother. Even though they offered their wealth to the Holy Mother with all devotion, she declined to accept it saying, “If you attain a pure character and become perfect spiritually, that will be my wealth. If one realizes the Essence within himself, he will see It in all. The whole world will become his own.” One night, a man from a neighbouring house woke up Ganga in order to borrow his torch (flashlight). The man's daughter was suffering from an acute asthma attack and had to be rushed off to the hospital in the dark. After a few hours, the man came back and returned the torch. The next morning Ganga told Mother about the incident and added that he had felt like breaking the man’s head for disturbing his sleep. The Mother scolded him saying, “What kind of spiritual aspirant ar you? What
99: have you gained by leading a spiritual life for so many years before coming here? Is this the fruit of that? Being a follower of the Path of Knowledge as you seem to think you are, you should see everything as your own Self, if this is so, how could you be angry with that man? If your foot was pricked by a sharp Ancient non-dualistic philosophy of life which expresses the essence of the Vedas.The Mother Of Immortal Bliss 169 thorn, would you not feel the pain and be restless to remove it? Imagine that man’s anxiety to relieve his daughter of her suf­ fering. The pain and suffering of all living beings should be felt to be your own. Only then will your mind expand and become as the sky which accomodates all equally. For that, your mind should become innocent like a child’s and this is possible only through pure devotion to God.” Ganga replied in a mocking tone, “Devotion is not at all intellectually satisfying. To follow the Path of Devotion shows a certain weakness. Why all these emotional expres­ sions like weeping and singing? I cannot do that. Sri Ramana never prescribed the Path of Devotion. He would only pre­ scribe the Path of Knowledge to his devotees. I prefer the Path of Knowledge as it appeals to the intellect. It is more convinc­ ing.” Such was Ganga's misconception of devotion at the time of his coming to the Mother. xMother smilingly retorted, "I have just seen the fruit of your practice of the Path of Knowledge. If this is the result, then you need not bother about leading a life of sacrifice and renunciation. You may as well enjoy all the pleasures of the senses! Did you read all the writings by and about Sri Ramana? If not, then please do so, for there are many works which are full of devotion. In fact, he himself was an embodiment of devotion to Lord Arunachala. Even the mere mention of that Name would bring tears of Divine Love to his eyes. Devotion is not an indication of mental weakness as you seem to think. It is the greatest achievement that a human can attain. It is perceiving God in all beings equally; it is pure love of Selfless Existence. Son, you should cultivate love within yourself.” Not convinced by the Holy Mother’s words, Ganga went to Tiruvannamalai. Much to his surprise, he happened to come across a devotional work of Sri Ramana’s. Recollecting the Mother’s words, he was filled with an overwhelming feel-170 MATA AMRJTANANDAMAYI ing of love and began weeping. He prayed to the Mother to call him back to her Holy Presence. It was at this time that the Mother, knowing his mental state, wrote a letter asking Ganga to return. Clearly realizing her greatness, he surren­ dered himself at her feet with all humility. Madhu had met many saints before coming to the Holy Mother, but when he saw the Mother for the
100: first time he felt as though he had come to the end of his journey. Dedicating his heart and soul to the Mother, Madhu began to collect all the extant commentaries on the Srimad Bhagavad Gita and to translate them into French for the spiritual benefit of French devotees. Inspired by the Holy Mother, he spread her spiri­ tual mission in his native land of Reunion and built a beautiful Ashram there dedicated to her. With the blessings of the Holy Mother, he has been instrumental in bringing many people to the spiritual path. In those days, the Holy Mother spent most of her nights outdoors. Therefore, everyone preferred to sleep beneath the coconut palms in the sand. Even if the Mother happened to take rest in the hut, by the middle of the night she would get up and walk outside to he down on the open ground. It re­ mained a fact that the Holy Mother slept very little, ate very little and gave unstintingly of herself. Even after sitting in the temple receiving the devotees three times a week for the entire night, she always made time during the day to receive the devotees and to instruct the spiritual aspirants who sought her guidance. In the beginning stages, Nealu and Gayatri had a lot of lan­ guage problems. They always sought the help of Balu to con­ verse with the Holy Mother, but before long, they slowly be­ gan picking up the Mother's language, Malayalam. During this period, Balu was very lucky to serve the Holy Mother as there was nobody else to look after her needs. One day, Sugunanandan rudely remarked that he was un­ willing to feed the ‘saippus' (foreigners). Thereafter, Gayatri began preparing food in the hut for the Holy Mother, Nealu,The .Mother Of Immortal Bliss 171 Balu and herself. The Mother ate almost nothing. Sometimes at the insistence of Nealu or Balu she would eat a little, forname sake only. One day, Nealu continued insisting that the Mother should eat some food. Finally, the Mother said, “Yes, I will eat. Bring something.” Without delay, Nealu brought a plateful of food for the Mother. Strangely enough, she fin­ ished everything in the twinkling of an eye. Nealu again served food to the Mother. That also was consumed in no time. The Mother looked expectantly at Nealu without mov­ ing from her seat. More food was served. This was also eaten. The Mother ate and ate but whatever was served seemed to leave her unsatisfied! Nealu and the others looked at each other in astonishment. More food was brought from the nearby teashop. That also was consumed by the Mother with­ out delay. Nealu was exhausted! He turned pale. Never again did he insist that the Holy Mother eat! During this period, family conflicts again cropped up. Only two months had passed since the marriage of Sugun- amma when Sugunanandan hastily arranged the marriages of his other two daughters. Without anybody's consent, he fixed the marriage
101: of his eldest daughter Kasturi. Even the Holy Mother was informed only after Sugunanandan's ac­ ceptance had been conveyed to the bridegroom's party. The problem was, how to conduct the marriage withoutany money? Sugunanandan had no income and there was nomoney in the temple. As was his habit, Sugunanandanstepped out of the picture at this juncture. The Mother wasunperturbed. Witnessing this, Balu became very sad andasked, “Mother, what is the plan? How to conduct the mar­riage?” Nealu told, “Mother, I will give whatever I have. It isthe duty of the disciple to look after and discharge the respon­sibilities of his Guru. I have nothing to call my own; whateverI have is Mother's. Therefore, please perform Kasturi's mar­riage with the money which I have at my disposal.” The Holy Mother replied, “After the marriage, the girls will lead a worldly life. What you have is the wealth meant for172 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI spirituality. That must be spent only for righteous purposes. If it is given to worldly people, they will incur sin. That will affect us and our path as well. If it is God who made the father fix the marriage, let God conduct it. We do not have to pay attention to it. Sugunanandan is not much bothered by it; then why should we worry? Children, we do not have to be perplexed about the matter.” Though Sugunanandan was in a haste to fix the marriage, when it came to the more important details like the finances, one could find him standing on the sidelines. The Mother began organizing the needful without uttering a word. See­ ing this, Balu became pained at heart and told the Mother, “I will bring my inheritance from my house.” The Holy Mother was in complete disagreement. Then Balu wrote to some close devotees requesting financial assistance. Later, when the Mother came to know of this, she scolded him for this action saying, “Son, let us confront it calmly. There is nothing to be excited about.” At last, everything was ready except one item, five thou­ sand rupees. This amount was absolutely necessary to meet the expenses of the wedding. After a few days passed, a cheque for Rs.5000 was received from an anonymous donor from Madras who had recently heard about the Holy Mother. Thus, in mid-September of 1980, Kasturi was married. Not three months had passed when Sugunanandan fixed Sajani's marriage. That done, he repeated his disappearing act and left the difficult part to the Holy Mother. The respon­ sibility for accumulating enough money to give as dowry, to pay the expense of the wedding ceremony and to craft the bride’s gold ornaments all fell on the Holy Mother’s shoul­ ders. Balu no longer felt sad, he felt angry! The Mother was also displeased by Sugunanandan’s lack of discrimination. Though annoyed, the Mother remained unruffled and dis­ charged the task with efficacy. The bridegroom’s party asked for more gold. As usual, money remained a big question mark. The Holy Mother was very particular that
102: not a penny beThe Mother Of Immortal Bliss 173 spent from what had been put aside for righteous purposes. Nor would she allow any money to be borrowed. Then what to do? At this juncture, Kasturi returned to Idamannel from her husband’s place due to some difference of opinion. Coming to know of the need for more gold she told, “See, for the time being you can take my ornaments to conduct Sajani’s mar­ riage. It is enough if you return them later.” Now, everything except one necklace and one ring was at hand. Two days be­ fore the marriage these items were yet to be had; still the Mother was calm and detached as ever. The next morning when the Bhava Darshan was over Gayatri, who was cleaning the temple, noticed a small package lying among the offer­ ings. Opening it, what was her surprise to find a necklace and ring fitting the exact description of what was needed for the marriage! Even the style of the ornaments was the same as had been selected a month ago! What other proof was needed to show that the Divine Will arranges for everything? However, this was not the end of the difficulties which plagued this final wedding. Some among the local devotees raised an objection with Sugunanandan. Why had he fixed a marriage alliance with those who had previously been ene­ mies? Were the sons of the well-wishers and devotees not good enough for him? Some young men who had been close friends of Subhagan had wished to marry Sugunanandan's daughters. Now they also turned against him. Thus, when enemies became relatives those who were friends became enemies. They came to quarrel with Sugunanandan and con­ spired to create obstacles for Sajani’s marriage. Hoping to annul the marriage, they spread scandalous stories and sent the same to the bridegroom. Even the day before the wedding everyone was full of doubt whether or not the marriage would be performed. On the day of the marriage, the Holy Mother took the brahmacharis to the neighbouring house. For the two previ­ ous marriages she had done the same. This was for the benefit of the brahmacharis who should not attend such ceremonies.174 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI The Mother explained, "An aspirant should not take part in marriage ceremonies or funerals. At the former, everyone will be thinking of the marriage, which is a bondage. At the later, the grief is about the loss of a mortal being. In both cases, the participants are dwelling on the non-eternal. These thought waves will be harmful to a seeker. The worldly vibrations will enter the subconscious mind. The seeker will become restless for un­ real things.” Thus, the main impediment for the brahmacharis to take up permanent residence in the Holy Mother’s presence had been removed. All three of Sugunanandan's daughters were now married. Not only that. The miscreants and rationalists, accepting utter failure, retreated one by one. Some among them understood that their irrational actions were meaning­ less
103: and left the organization forever. The remaining mem­ bers began fighting among themselves and thus the ‘Commit­ tee to Stop Blind Beliefs’ was totally dissolved. Those who came to fight against Truth and Righteousness became the cause of their own destruction. These developments marked the beginning of a new phase in the Holy Mother's spiritual service to relieve and uplift ailing humanity. Mother’s attitude towards all the trials and tribulations which she suffered from her relatives and miscreants over the years is unique. One day she narrated, "It was their misguided conceptions that made them speakand behave the way they did and also because they could notrealise the significance and purport of spiritual life. Suchbeing the case, why should we be angry with them or dislikethem? It would be our ignorance to do so and as a result ourminds would only get polluted. Look at these fresh roses.How beautiful they are ! What a fine fragrance they exude!But what do we give them to make them grow? Just a little usedtea leaves and cow dung! What a vast difference betweenThe Mother Of Immortal Bliss 175 these beautiful flowers and the manure given to them! Com­ pared to their beauty and fragrance, is this manure befitting them? Likewise, impediments are the fertilizer which makes us grow stronger spiritually. These obstacles will all help our hearts to blossom fully. Chirping at night is the nature of crickets but that sound never disturbs anybody’s sleep. Like­ wise, to create trouble is the nature of the ignorant. We must therefore pray to God to forgive them and to lead them to the right path. Dedicate everything to God and He will look after you.” The Mother of Immortal Bliss It now became possible for the first group of brahmacharis to settle at the feet of the Holy Mother due to the relative peace of mind that Sugunanandan enjoyed since the marriage of his daughters. Despite the fact that there were no accomo­ dations, due to the brahmacharis’ keen interest to be in the company of the Holy Mother, they took no notice of the fact that food, clothing and shelter were minimal. Most of the time, they had to live outdoors and sleep on the bare ground without even a mat. Whatever was received came unasked and was shared amongst themselves. Having no money, if any of them had to go somewhere, they would walk. Even though they had only one set of clothes each, somehow they learned to manage. Feeling a bit depressed one day that his only set of clothes was dirty and worn, one of the brahmacharis complained to the Mother about the lack of necessities. The Mother replied, “Son, do not ask for such small things from God. Surrender yourself at His Feet and He will give you whatever you really need. ” The Holy Mother had lived that way herself and there­ fore spoke only from her own experience. The very next day a devotee brought a new set of
104: clothes for all the brahmacharis although he had been unaware of the situation at the time. These boys received an all-round training in renunciation due to the austere circumstances which they lived in during176 MATA AMR IT AN AND AM AY I the early days of the Ashram. In order to give them courage, the Holy Mother told them, “If you can withstand this here, you can be at home anywhere. If you can overcome any ad­ verse situation now, then you will easily cope with any crisis or challenge of life.” As the number of devotees and resident brahmacharis went on increasing and the lack of facilities stayed the same, the idea to formally incorporate as an Ashram was born. However, the situation did not look very promising. The Holy Mother had neither land nor wealth at her disposal. Even the land where Nealu had built the hut belonged to Sugunanandan. Although he had given permission for Nealu, Balu and Gayatri to stay at Idamannel permanently, Sugunanandan had never entertained the idea that his home would become a future Ashram. The thought of accomo­ dating more and more people did not hold his fancy either. Once when the Holy Mother was discussing the idea of an Ashram, he angrily made his point very clear, “What is this! Do we have anv wealth or riches here? How to run an Ashram? Where shall we (the family) go if this becomes an Ashram? No! I shall not agree to register an Ashram here!” Initially, the Holy Mother was not in favour of the ideatobecome a formal Ashram either. When some amongstthedevotees approached her with the proposal, shereplied,“Mother has heard a lot about ‘Ashram’. Motherdoes notneed an Ashram. Is it not a bondage? Haven’t youseen thepalmist walking with the caged parrot, i.e. beingbound foranother person’s purpose? At last. Mother’ssituation willalso become like that. I cannot do that. Motherhas her ownfreedom. There should be no obstacle for that.” However, as the flow of devotees and disciples swelled, it was not long before the need of an organised Ashram became an unavoidable necessity. Furthermore, the Holy Mother’s foreign disciples, according to the law of the land, were pro­ hibited from staying in a private house for any appreciable length of time. At this point the Holy Mother herself became convinced of the need for a government approved spiritualThe Mother Of Immortal Bliss 177 centre. The Mother’s opinion was sought as to how to pro­ ceed. She mischievously replied, “Anyhow, the family members will not establish an Ashram. Their samskara14 is different. Let us not wait for their permission; they will never co-operate. But we may have to hear a little of their scold­ ings!” Thus, on the 6th of May in the year of 1981, with a view to preserving and propagating the ideals and teachings of the Holy Mother, the Mata Amritanandamayi Mission was founded and registered under the Travancore-Cochin State Literary and Charitable Act of 1955, at Quilon, Kerala, South India. From this
105: time onwards, the Holy Mother offi­ cially adopted the name 'Mata Amritanandamayi’ which was given to her by one of her brahmacharin sons. Already being the Mother of Immortal Bliss, as her name denotes, the name was indeed befitting. Around this time, one of the brahmacharins, being in need of some scriptural books, requested Mother to select a num­ ber for him from a lottery in which the winner would be pre­ sented with some books. Mother told him, “Why crave for such things? You will soon receive plenty of books.” It was shortly after this incident that Nealu, who came to stay per­ manently with Mother from Tiruvannamalai, decided to shift his library of more than 2000 books in English and vari­ ous Indian languages from that place to the Ashram in Val­ lickavu. Thus, the Ashram Library came into being. On August 27, 1982 a Vedanta Vidyalaya (School) was started in order to impart traditional Vedantic and Sanskrit knowledge to the residents of the Ashram. All the same. Mother always reminds the brahmacharins of the importance of meditation instead of mere book learning. The Ashram routine consists of six to eight hours of meditation for all the residents. Those who would like to devote all of their time for meditation are by all means encouraged and there are a few such in the Ashram. Mother says, 4 Culture or mental tendency.178 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI “Scriptures are just like signboards. They are only a means but not an end in themselves. The end is something beyond them. A student of agriculture knows how to sow the seeds, when and how to put the fertilizer, how to get nd of pests and prevent their return, etc. Likewise, study of scriptures gives us directions as to how to do our spintual practices.” A word should be said about the vast change which has come over the Holy Mother's family and the villagers. Reali­ sing her to be divine, they now feel proud of the fact that they are related to her or living in the same village. Sugunanandan and Damayanthi often wonder what meritorious acts they performed in their past lives in order to become the "parents’ of the Divine Mother Herself! They are now exemplary householders and lovingly play the role of a father and mother to all of the brahmacharins who stay in the Ashram, regarding them as their own children. Today, Mata Amritanandamayi Mission is a growing spin­ tual centre headed by the Divine Mother who is very particu­ lar to see that it is run according to the ancient traditions of this holy land of India. All the Ashram work is attended to by the residents themselves who each put in at least one hour of work each day for the maintenance of the Ashram, cooking, cleaning, tending the cows, etc. The Ashram is looked upon by Mother’s many devotees as their spiritual home and as a fertile field where noble spiritual qualities can be abundantly cultivated, and the
106: fruit of God-Realisation reaped. Responding to the repeated requests of her children from abroad, the Holy Mother made her first world tour during May-August 1987. She travelled widely in the U.S.A, and Europe. The impact was wonderful. The Holy Mother in­ spired and transformed many people who experienced her unique spiritual charm and universal love on a vast scale. In December 1987, the Holy Mother visited Reunion Island and Mauritius at the request of the Mata Amritanandamayi Mission Centre, which has been functioning there well since 1985 under the leadership of one of her disciples. Subse-Ihc Mother Ot Immortal Bliss 179 quently, the Holy Mother made two more world tours in 1988 and 1989. To those blessed souls who find their way to Mother’s Holy Presence, she graciously advises them; “Beholding a block of stone, a master sculptor will see only the beautiful form latent in it ignoring its rough exterior. Likewise, a God-Realised Soul sees only the ever-shining Atman or Self in all without distinction, ignoring the external differences. A drunkard cannot propagate prohibition of liq­ uor. He should himself abstain from drinking first and then onlv can he ask others to do the same. Likewise, mv children, only after you yourselves become morally and spiritually per­ fect and behold Divinity in all, can you teach others to become so.” Let this life story of the Holy Mother be concluded with her loving call to the entire human race: Come quickly my darling children,you who are the DivineEssence of 'OM'.Removing all sorrows,grow to be adorableand merge withthe sacred "OM'!PART 2 The Meaning Of The Divine BhavasThe Divine Bhavas of the Holy Mother as Krishna and Devi is a subject which needs a deep and analytical study even though it is, in truth, beyond the reach of the human intellect. This would help the readers to get a glimpse into the Holy Mother’s infinite spiritual power. Responding to the sincere call of the devotee, God or a Perfect Master slowly reveals His infinite attributes within His devotee’s heart lotus. When the process of seIf-purification becomes more and more intense, the Guru's greatness, which is nothing but the True Nature of the disciple or devotee, will be gradually revealed by the Grace of the Master. Grace, of course, is the first and foremost thing which one needs to understand the significance of the Holy Mother’s Divine Moods. The Great Masters of India have classified the concept of Divine Incarnations under three main categories: l)Purna Avatara (full or perfect), 2)Amsa Avatara (partial manifesta­ tion) and 3)Avesa Avatara (temporary overshadowing by divine power). The word ‘Avatara’ means coming down or descent. A Puma Avatara is the descent of the nameless, form­ less and immutable Supreme Energy assuming a human form and manifesting infinite power without any limitations. The intention of such a One will be to restore and preserve right­ eousness (dharma) and uplift humanity by making it aware of the higher Self. Amsa Avatara is the coming down of God while partially manifesting
107: some of His power in order to ful­ fill a particular purpose or goal. The Incarnations of Lord Vishnu as Vamana (the Dwarf) and Narasimha (Man-lion) are typical examples of Amsa Avataras. Avesa Avatara is entirely different from the above two types of Incarnations. This is the temporary visitation or possession by divine beings who use the body of some people in order to fulfill certain tasks. Lord Vishnu’s Incarnation as Parasurama as depicted in the epic Srimad Bhagavata, comes under this heading. Here, the Lord entered the body of Para­ surama who was a great warrior in order to destroy the cruel kshatriya kings who had become very arrogant and egoistic. Soon after fulfilling this task the power left him. It is said that184 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Sri Rama, another Incarnation of Lord Vishnu, took the di­ vine power back from Parasurama while returning to Ayodhya after his marriage with Sita. It is said that demons and ghosts will possess the body of people who are mentally weak. People who are predomi­ nantly virtuous and good in nature (sattvic) will get pos­ sessed by devas (minor gods), those endowed with creativity and vigour (rajasic) will be possessed by celestial beings (those beings inferior to minor gods) and people whose na­ ture is full of darkness and obscurity (tamas) will be possessed by evil spirits. It is also told that in the body of a few rare souls who are extremely pure. Divine Power will manifest for a short period of time. This is why Parasurama is considered to be an Avesa Avatara. The following is an illustration which might help the reader to gain insight into the Holy Mother’s Divine Bhavas. Once upon a time when Lord Krishna was living in Dwaraka he cherished a desire to see his dear devotee Hanuman. His vehicle Garuda, the king of birds, was sent as his messenger to Kadali Vanam where Hanuman was residing, but Hanuman refused to go. He explained, “I won't go to see anyone other than my Lord Rama". When the reply of Hanuman was con­ veyed to Lord Sri Krishna, He once again sent the bird chief to Hanuman saying, “You go and tell him that Lord Sri Rama with His holy consort Sita have come to Dwaraka and They would like to see Hanuman." While Garuda was fetching Hanuman, certain happen­ ings were taking place in Dwaraka. Lord Krishna, by His mere Will, assumed the form of Lord Rama who lived aeons ago. Rukmini, Krishna’s consort, became Sita. By this time, Ha­ numan arrived in Dwaraka and having seen his beloved Sri Rama and Sita and having offered worship to Them, returned to his abode. Though Sri Rama was also one among the Incarnations of Lord Vishnu, He lived in Ayodhya thousands of years before Sri Krishna's lifetime. Yet Hanuman, the great devotee of Lord Rama, did not doubt how Lord Rama and Sita couldThe Meaning Of The Divine Bhavas 185 appear in Dwaraka even though the all-knowing Hanuman
108: was fully aware of the fact that Krishna was the Lord of Dwar­ aka. Certainly Hanuman knew that no one except Krishna could manifest Rama Bhava. What really happened was that Hanuman was determined to use this opportunity to see his Lord with Sita in human form once again. Lord Krishna, the servant of His devotees, happily fulfilled His great devotee's wish and blessed him. Only a Purna Avatara can become one with any other God or Goddess. Being such an Avarara, Krishna easily mani­ fested Rama Bhava. One day. Lord Krishna asked His wives including Satyabhama, one among His dear ones, to assume Sita Bhava, but none were able to. Finally Rukmini, an Incar­ nation of Goddess Lakshmi, effortlessly assumed Sita Bhava. In the case of an Avesa Avatara, godly powers enter into a particular person and after fulfilling the intended goal, re­ treat. This was not the case with Sri Krishna and Rukmini. What Lord Krishna had done was to manifest Rama Bhava or the attributes of Rama which were already potential in Him. There is a similar incident in the life of Lord Chaitanya of Bengal. One day Pandit Srivasa, an ardent devotee of Lord Nara- simha, was performing his usual chanting (mantra japa) while sitting in his family shrine room. Suddenly there was a knock on the door. “Who is that?" the pandit asked. “Behold the Beloved Deity who is being worshipped by you," came the reply. Pandit Srivasa opened the door and beheld Lord Chaitanya standing before him on the threshold of the house in a divine mood. He entered the shrine room and sat on the seat which was specially made for worship. The pandit saw the Lord Narasimha shining within Lord Chaitanya and with great devotion worshipped Him in Lord Chaitanya’s form. Lord Chaitanya blessed the pandit's family by permitting all of them to take part in the worship. After everyone had received his blessings. Lord Chaitanya fell down unconscious. On regaining his external awareness after a few moments, he asked the pandit, “What happened? I186 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI cannot remember anything. Did I utter anything wrong?” With all humility, Srivasa prostrated before his Lord and said, “O Bhagavan, please do not delude this humble servant of Yours anymore. By Your Grace this humble servant was able to see who You are!” Hearing this. Lord Chaitanya gra­ ciously smiled in a gesture of confirmation. Many such inci­ dents are there is Lord Chaitanya's life of his giving darshan to his devotees in diverse bhavas. From the above illustrations one can clearly understand what Bhava Darshan is. Bhava Darshan is the manifestation of different Isvara Bhavas or Divine Moods by an Incarnation of God according to the wishes of the devotees. Anandamayi Ma who lived in Bengal used to manifest Krishna and Kali Bhavas while singing bhajans. These Bhavas manifested by Incarnations took place only on certain occasions to fulfill a particular end, especially in reponse to the ardent desire of their devotees. Moreover, they would
109: last only for a short time. The Holy Mother Amritanandamayi manifests the Di­ vine Moods three nights a week lasting for long periods of 10 to 12 hours depending upon the number of devotees present for Darshan. As far as the Holy Mother is concerned, this is her way of serving humanity, plunged as it is in the deep quag­ mire of worldliness. Lord Chaitanya is said to have had two bhavas-that of a devotee in which mood he would be most frequently seen, and Bhagavat Bhava during which he would reveal his real state of Self-Abidance. Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa also had the same. It is said that he even developed a small growth like a tail during the period of his spiritual practices in Hanuman Bhava. During the Holy Mother's Krishna and Devi Bhavas she brings out That which is within her and manifests those Divine Beings in order to bless her devotees. The Holy Mother once said regarding the same, “Mother is not manifesting even an infinitesimal part of her spiritual power during the Bhavas. If is were to be mani­ fested as it is, no one could come near!” She continues, “All The Meaning Of The Divine Bhavas 187 the deities of the Hindu Pantheon, who represent the num­ berless aspects of the One Supreme Being exist within us. A Divine Incarnation can manifest any of them by mere will for the good of the world. Krishna Bhava is the manifestation of the Purusha or Pure Being aspect and Devi Bhava is the mani­ festation of the Eternal Feminine, the Creatrix, the active principle of the Impersonal Absolute. Here is a crazy girl who puts on the garb of Krishna and after some time that of Devi, but it is within this crazy girl that both exist. However, it should be remembered that all objects having a name or form are mere mental projections. Why decorate an elephant? Why should a lawyer wear a black coat or why does a policeman wear a uniform and a cap? All these are merely external aids meant to create a certain impression. In a like manner. Mother dons the garb of Krishna and Devi in order to give strength to the devotional attitude of the people coming for Darshan. The Atman or Self that is in me is also within you. If you can realise that Indivisible Principle that is ever shining in you, you will become That.” Even now' there are some people who believe that Lord Krishna and Devi are visiting the Holy Mother’s body three nights a week at the end of which They go out of her body. This misconception is due to the lack of correct understanding about the Holy Mother's Divine Moods. These Divine Bha­ vas are nothing but the external revelation of her incessant Oneness with the Supreme. 'This has nothing to do with pos­ session or Divine Grace as is commonly understood. Answering the queries of the devotees, the Holy Mother has explained
110: many points relating to the Bhavas: Devotee: Many devotees say that Mother is the same during the Divine Bhavas and at other times. If this is so, then what is the significance of the Bhavas? Mother: During the time of the Bhava Darshan Mother will remove two or three layers (veils, so to say), so that the devotees can obtain a glimpse of the Supreme. Different188 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI people have different kinds of belief. Mother's intention is to somehow help the people approach God. Some are interested only if they see Mother in the costume of Devi or Krishna. Not only that...very few people know anything about spiritual­ ity’. Some people find it difficult ro believe Mother’s words during ordinary times, but if Mother says the same thing dur­ ing the Devi Bhava they will believe. Devotee: Mother, is there any particular time for manifesting this Bhava? Mother: No, there is not. It can be manifested at any time. Mere will is enough. Devotee: Mother, why do you wear these costumes of Krishna and Devi? Mother: It will help the people to remember what Bhava is. Child, each attire is important in its own way. We are naked when we are born. Later, according to each country’ and social custom, people adopt different kinds of dress. Whatever the dress, the person is the same. In this age people give much importance to dress. Mother will make this point clear through an anecdote. One man was cutting down a tree which was growing by the side of a road. Another man who hap­ pened to see him doing this said, “Don’t cut down that tree!.It is wrong to do so, it is against the law." The man not only re­ fused to stop cutting but also scolded him severely. The per­ son who tried to prevent the hooligan from cutting the tree was a policeman. He departed but soon returned in his official dress. Even from a distance the mere sight of the policeman’s cap was enough to make the hooligan flee without looking back. See the different impact created when he came in ordi­ nary and then official dress. Therefore, special attire is needed to teach ignorant people. Likewise, the costumes of Krishna and Devi Bhavas. Some people who still feel dissatis­ fied even after talking to Mother for hours will feel fully con­ tent after conversing with her only for a coulplc of seconds during Bhava Darshan. They feel peaceful after having told all their worries directly to God.”The Meaning Of The Divine Bhavas 189 Al! Incarnations are unique in nature. One cannot say thatKrishna was greater than Rama or that Rama was greater thanBuddha. Each of Them had Their own task to fulfill andadopted befitting ways to uplift humanity. But this does notmean that They had different visions of life. Their actionscannot be assessed using the measuring rod of our limitedintellect and logic. We can get perhaps a glimpse of theirgreatness through pure intuition born of spiritual practice.The spiritual
111: experiences gained by thousands of devoteesthrough the Holy Mother throws much light on this GreatSoul’s inexplicable spiritual power. In the following pages wewill share with the reader some of the divine experiences of theHoly Mother’s devotees as related by them... * * * * *PART 3 Experiences Of DevoteesEXPERIENCES OF DEVOTEES Dr.D.N.Srivastava, Scientific Officer at the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre in Bombay writes about his experi­ ence with the Holy Mother: “In my student days I had studied the life of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in which there were vivid descriptions of the flow of divine love from his lips, from his face and from his actions when he used to utter the Holy Names. For many years I had aspired to see someone with that much saturation of devotion. I came across many saints, some of them really sin­ cere in their pursuit, but that intensity of radiant bliss was not found. But when I came in contact with Mata Amritanandamayi and heard her songs the words of which I could not under­ stand fully, even then I felt that I had found what I had been looking for since years. Even just by sitting beside her I could feel the spiritual current which is experienced in deep medita­ tion. Her address to the Supreme by various Names reveals kaleidoscopically one after the other facets of the Infinite. Her simple explanation to many complex questions shows the clarity of her vision of the Reality. Her spontaneously oozing words impart wings to one's intuition to fly to the world of inner light where there are no doubts. Even in this age of hypocrisy the number of dedicated seek­ ers is not small, but the complete dissolution in the Universal Love which Mata Amritanandamayi symbolises is an ex­ tremely rare phenomenon.” ***** Sheeja is a 7 year old girl. That year during Lord Krishna's birthday celebrations, Takkali came to the Ashram. The Holy Mother, who was standing at the boat jetty, took her by the hand and walked with her to the Ashram. Her dream came true. Even without
112: being asked. Mother had understood her innocent wish and fulfilled it. God knows and fulfills the longings of the pure at heart. ****** Nambisseri Bhargavi Amma was an ardent devotee of Lord Krishna. Suddenly she realised the meaning of Krishna's words. The portrait of Sri Krishna which she worshipped for a long time was then locked up in the cup­ board. Some time ago when she had gone to stay with her daughter she had locked up the portrait in the cupboard to protect it from the attack of insects and worms. Bhargavi hadExperiences Of Devotees 195 forgotten to take it out from the cupboard and to start her worship when she returned. It was sitting in there for a long time. Hearing these words and understanding her mistake she burst into tears and sought forgiveness from Krishna. Now Bhargavi was fully convinced that there was no differ­ ence between Lord Krishna, her Beloved Deity, and the Holy Mother. ***** The Holy Mother always used to be in the company of small children with whom she would play and spend her time mer­ rily like a small child herself. Hearing the verdict of the doctors that she was dead and overcome by grief and weeping aloud, the grandmother came straight to the shrine and laid the dead body of the child on the sacred seat on which Mother would sit during darshan times. Mother, who was then absorbed in singing devotional songs in another house, immediately felt restless and stopping the singing abruptly, hastened to the temple. When she reached there, she found the old grand­ mother weeping and wailing and noticed the dead body of the little girl on the seat. "Oh Mother, please save the child” begged the wailing lady. By and by, the small girl opened her eyes and gradually revived, ’fears of joy rolled down the checks of the grandmother's face and she embraced the Holy Mother again and again. ***** Bindu, a fourteen year old girl and her parents were very devoted to the Holy Mother. It was daytime and I was standing in the front verandah of the house. Suddenly a woman in white clothes was seen ap­ proaching the house through the front gate. I have clearly seen Her Experiences Of Devotees 197 coming up to the front yard of the house and then suddenly disappear. For clarification, he asked her how this could happen. The Holy Mother smilingly replied, “Who knows? It might be that daughter's innocent faith!” ***** Nothing is impossible for God. By God's Grace the dumb speak, the deaf hear and the lame climb mountains. Both Mr. Ramankutty, a highschool headmaster who resides in 198 MATA AM R IT AN AN DAM A YI Kayamkulam about 15 miles from the Ashram, and his wife Saradamba have unshakeable faith in the Holy Mother and have had a number of experiences revealing her ineffable spiritual power. Mother told them that within a certain period of time they would get all of the stolen articles back which in fact hap­ pened. Through them Ramankutty came to know about the Holy Mother. This vast change in her condition made her parents into ardent devotees of the Holy Mother. Other miraculous incidents took place in the life of Ramankutty’ and his wife after coming to Amma. 115: look after everything. Only I should be offered My usual banana and milk everyday in the shrine room.” Before leaving the house, the couple cut down a big unripened banana bunch from their vegetable garden and, after covering it with a sack, hung it from the ceiling of the store room to ripen. After the pilgrimage everyone returned to the Ashram. It was Bhava Darshan day. Both the husband and wife decided to stay for the Darshan and then return home. When Ramankutty approached Amma who was in Krishna Bhava, she smilingly said, “I have tasted all the bananas. Don't beat the children. I didn't eat any of them but just licked them all.Experiences Of Devotees 199 ’’Unable to understand the meaning of Mother’s words, Ramankutty left for home. After reaching there he remem­ bered about the banana bunch kept for ripening. He took the storeroom key from its hiding place and opened the room. What he saw there was astonishing. The sack used to cover the bananas was lying on the floor and all of the bananas were on it forming a perfect circle! How could this happen? The sack was securely tied around the bunch with a rope. Thinking that it must be some mischief done by his children he called them and questioned them. Inspire of their professions of inno­ cence, he tied their hands behind their backs and beat them. During the next Darshan day when Ramankutty entered the shrine room where Mother was standing in Krishna Bhava, he saw her standing there as if with her hands tied be­ hind her back. She said to him, "Go ahead and beat Me. I am ready to bear it. I am the one who plucked the bananas from the bunch because the children didn't offer Me My usual ba­ nana and milk every’ day!" Mother was standing in exactly the same pose as Ramankutty’’s children had been in when he had beaten them. Both he and his wife shed tears of joy thinking of their great good fortune in having the Holy Mother as their God, Guru and Mother all in one. ***** Ramamurti of Madurai in Tamil Nadu was a truck driver. Nine years back, his five year old daughter suddenly dis­ appeared. All of his efforts to find her ended in vain. As he narrated his sorrowful tale to the Holy Mother, he heaved deep sighs. Lovingly consoling him she said, “Don’t worry, you will get your daughter back." He believed in her words. Now and then, whenever he would come to Kerala he would never forget to visit the Holy Mother. One day Mother gave200 . MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Ramamurti some flowers and said, “Within a month you will get your daughter back. ” One day during his travels, Ramamurti stopped his truck
116: in a market place in Madras. He saw a few people gathered around a girl of about 14 or 15 years of age who was crying over her lost money which she had kept for purchases. Some people tried to console her saying that they would themselves give her the lost amount but she refused to accept it. The poor girl continued weeping. Ramamurti lovingly asked her name. On hearing it, he be­ came surprised. It was the same name as his missing daugh­ ter’s. He looked very’ closely at her face and was overwhelmed with joy to find that it was indeed his missing daughter. He said, “Child, I will take you back to your home. Don't worry’ about the money. I will tell them all about it. ” The girl agreed and so Ramamurti drove the truck to the girl's house. It was a big house owned by a rich landlord. With great hesitation Ramamurti told all about his past to the landlord. After hearing the story’, with great feeling the landlord re­ plied, “About ten years ago while I was returning home in my car after a short journey I saw a crying child standing by the side of the road. I asked her several questions but she would neither tell me where she came from or how she became lost. She only told me her name by which we continued to call her. She was alone in that place and I was utterly ignorant of her place of residence. Since I had no children, I brought her home with me and raised her as my own child. When there is no one to do the daily purchases we send her to the market and that is how you found her there. ” Understanding Ramamurti’s feelings, the landlord hap­ pily gave him permission to take back his daughter even though he was sad to lose his adopted daughter. Ramamurti was overjoyed. Mother's words turned our to be true as he got his daughter back before the completion of the month. From that time onwards his faith in Mother became deep and firm. Experiences Of Devotees 201 Rukmini Amma resides in Quilon about 35 kilometres from the Holy Mother’s Ashram. Due to high blood pressure the right side of her body became completely paralysed and she became bedridden in 1978. She underwent all kinds of Allopathic and Ayurvedic treatment for 3 years but to no avail. Large sums of money had to be spent for these treat­ ments and medicines but finally she, her husband Achutan Pillai and their relatives became utterly disappointed. It was at this time that they heard of the Holy Mother and decided to take Rukmini Amma to the Ashram. After bring­ ing her in a car, two strong men carried her into the Ashram on a chair. As is Mother's custom, she gave her some sacred ashes as prasad, blessed her and sent her away without uttering even a word about her disease. Both
117: the husband and the wife re­ turned to the house dissatisfied with the apparently fruitless journey. The car stopped a few yards away from their house. Carried by some people, Rukmini Amma was brought just in front of the house. Then followed an incredible incident. Standing up and pushing aside her attendants, Rukmini Amma walked into the house. Unable to believe their eyes, her husband and the others stood motionless. Their happi­ ness knew no bounds. Thenceforth, they became ardent devotees of the Holy Mother. Rukmini Amma slowly re­ gained her health. Seeing her, it is difficult to believe that she is the same one who was completely bedridden with paralysis. ***** Neelambaran is a pious man who is very devoted to the Holy Mother. He lives in a small village named Vawakkavu about 4 kilometres away from Mother’s Ashram. On almost all Darshan days he would come to the Ashram with a beauti­ ful garland of fragrant flowers for the Holy Mother. Though his faith in the Mother was unshakeable, his wife’s attitude was just the opposite. She would even scold him severely for going there.202 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI One day in 1975, Neelambaran was ploughing a field situ­ ated near a road. Some other workers were also working in the same field. Neelambaran was telling them about his difficul­ ties in making both ends meet. He said, “Today I don’t have even a penny to buy rice or other foodstuffs. ” Hearing this, the other workers teased him and mockingly said, “That girl in Vallickavu (the Holy Mother) will give you money. Wait till you get that! ” Everyone looked at Neelambaran and burst out laughing. Neelambaran turned pale and kept silent. After a few moments, a small girl was seen walking towards Neelambaran. As if she was a close relative of his she smiled at him and stretched out her right hand in which there was a twenty rupee note. Without a second thought, Neelambaran accepted the money. “See you,” the girl said and walked away without uttering another word. All the workers there had their own opinion about the probable source of the money. Some thought that it was a debt being repaid by someone while others thought that it was credit for some articles pledged. Yet, the fact was that none of them had ever seen her before. One of the workers asked, “Neelambaran, who was that girl?” Neelambaran hesitated. He was trying to figure out wherefrom the money had come. He stood there like a statue utterly confounded holding the money in one hand and think­ ing of the strange girl. His eyes were fixed on the path by which the girl had walked away. Hearing the question of his co-worker Neelambaran, as if in a dream said, “I am also wondering about the same.” All were surprised. The girl had gone away. Who she was and from where she came nobody knew. It remained a mystery for Neelambaran and his friends. The next Darshan day
118: when Neelambaran visited the Ashram, the Holy Mother lovingly and with much affection said to him, “Did the Mother Goddess give you a twenty rupee note yesterday? My child, it was Mother who came to you and gave you the money.” Neelambaran's heart overflowed with Divine Love and embracing the Holy Mother's feet, he shed tears like a child. *****Experiences Of Devotees 203 Francis Daniel was an assistant locomotive driver work­ ing in the Southern Railways in Quilon. He narrates a thrill­ ing experience of his after coming into contact with the Holy Mother. “Before meeting the Holy Mother Amritanandamayi, I was an atheist. Indulging in all kinds of wicked deeds, I led a degraded life. Becoming closely associated with the Railway Union politics, I actively participated in many strikes against the Government. This created a lot of enemies for me. They accused me of several crimes and created false evidence in order to trap me. Due to their position and influence in the ruling party I was suspended from service in March 1978. From then onwards, my life took a sharp turn for the worse. Having lost my job, I was thrown into the midst of dif­ ficulties coming from all directions. I found no way to make both ends meet. I lost all hope and led my life as an utterly dis­ appointed man. I was about to have a complete nervous break­ down when at that time I heard about the Holy Mother from some of the railway officers. Due to their persistance, I went to see her at her Ashram in Vallickavu. At the very first sight of the Mother's radiant form I fell at her feet and cried aloud, "O Mother, save me and grant me refuge!' With great affection Mother said, "Son, don’t worry’. iMother will straighten out everything. All the false evidence created against you will be burnt to ashes? Firmly believing in Mother's assurance, I peacefully sat in my home putting an end to all further efforts to get back my job. I continued visiting the Holy Mother and 11/2 years passed thus. On one fine morning there appeared an article in the daily newspaper about a train accident. The train was proceeding to Madras when the accident occured. A fire broke out and burnt many things to ashes. Several passengers were killed. While reading the paper I was not at all aware of the blessing which the Mother had thus showered upon me. Afterwards I came to know about the further details of the accident. It was on that same train that the officer who was appointed to enquire about the case lodged against me was 204 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI travelling carrying all the said false documents. In that acci­ dent all the false evidence and other forged materials were burnt to ashes. Owing to lack of proper documentation, the court suspended the petition filed against me and I regained my job. These incidents completely transformed my life and helped me to have
119: perfect faith in the unconditional power of God.” Dattan the Leper Boundless compassion is the nature of God. He discards none. His heart is like the expansive sky wherein the whole universe with all its beings can be accomodated. The never ending stream of His love and compassion embraces and en­ velopes every moving and unmoving creature in the world. All the great saints and sages of the past and present are embodiments of Divine Love and Compassion. One who meets the Holy Mother Amritanandamayi can experience this all-consuming and all-embracing love and compassion of God. The following narrative exemplifies this fact. Dattan was born and brought up in Perumpally near Kayamkulam in Kerala. When he was very young he became a victim of the terrible disease of leprosy. His own parents and relatives turned him out of the house. Finding no other way to earn a livelihood, Dattan became a beggar. He begged his food and spent his days and nights in a temple compound. In the course of time, his whole body was covered with infected wounds oozing pus. Dattan said, “My eyesight was almost lost. In the place of my eyes there were oply two small slits. The hair from my head fell out. Nobody would give me any food. I was not allowed to travel in a bus. Always I used to cover my body with a piece of cloth but even that would stick to my body and stink owing to the pus and blood oozing out from the putrid-smelling wounds. Even fellow beggars wouldn't allow me to eat and sleep near them. Insects and flys would always fly around me and disturb me. At the mere sight of me people would hold their nose and walk away. I neither heard aExperiences Of Devotees 205 loving word nor saw a compassionate face for years together. Everybody hated me and would spit on seeing my ugly look­ ing body and face. I led an utterly miserable and dejected life. All my hopes to live almost ended. It was at this juncture that I heard of the Holy Mother in Vallickavu. I went there during a Bhava Darshan day but no one would let me go inside to see the Mother. Full of abhor­ rence, the people said, “Go away, go away.” Such was the foul smell produced by my wounds. But the Holy Mother called me aside and consoled me and treated me like her own child just as she was treating everyone else.” It was a blood-curdling sight to watch the Holy Mother licking his pus-infected wounds, sucking the pus and blood from them. On all Bhava Darshan days, she would give him a bath pouring pots of water on his head. She would smear sacred ash all over his body and would treat him with the same amount of love, affection and compassion which she would show to other devotees. Witnessing the Holy Mother’s way of attending him, some devotees would vomit, some would faint,
120: others would watch with awe and reverence, and some would shed tears of love and devotion due to the realisation that they were sitting in the holy Presence of the Divine Mother Herself. Other than Her, who could show such equal love? The Holy Mother says about Dattan, “Who else is there to take care of him and to love him? Mother doesn’t see his external body. She sees only his heart. I cannot discard him. He is my son and I am his mother. Can a mother abandon her son?” Now Dattan is a changed man. Almost all his wounds have healed. The Holy Mother’s spittle is his divine medicine. His eyes have opened and he can see clearly now. Hair has also grown on his head. He can travel in a bus and people will talk to him and serve him food. Though the scars of the terrible disease still remain on Dattan's body there is no more blood or pus oozing from them nor is there any putrid smell. He can wear a shirt or a loincloth without it sticking to his body and causing him pain. Now he is happy. The Holy Mother has206 .MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI given him a new birth. His life has found meaning through her blessings. ***** Kuttappan Nair, a retired head constable says, “What I am narrating below is one of the numerous experiences I had with Mother. My family and myself have been householder devo­ tees of Mother for the past eight years. Five years ago, one night during Bhava Darshan Amma pointed out to me that some of the planets were not in a favourable position for me. On requesting Amma to tell me a solution for that, she advised me to observe a vow of silence on ail Saturdays. This was on a Thursday. On Friday, as I had been to the hospital to see my daughter who was in the maternity ward, I could not observe the vow of silence the next day, i.e. Saturday. That day, a hen which did not have any disease died in my house. The next Saturday also, unfortunately, I could not observe the vow of silence. That day, our pct puppy died. I could not observe si­ lence on the third Saturday also. That day a one and a half year old calf in our house died. It was then that I remembered about Amma’s instruction to observe the vow of silence. The next day, I proceeded to Vallickavu. When I pros­ trated before Amma during Bhava Darshan, Amma said, “You have not observed the vow which I advised. Already three deaths have taken place in your house. In future, if you do not observe the vow, more unpleasant happenings and misfortune will happen.” On the next Saturday, early morning after bath, I offered prayers at the Devi temple near our house, came back home and sat in meditation in the pooja room. Saturday being a holiday for schools, my grandchildren and nephew
121: were all home in the next room. When they started making noise, I scolded them forgetting about my vow. After that, I contin­ ued my vow of silence. I spent the entire day in the pooja room except for going out four times for breakfast, lunch, tea and supper.Experiences Of Devotees 207 The next day I went to Vallickavu. During Bhava Darshan, Amma caught hold of my ear and sternly rebuked me saying “Is this how you observe silence?” I asked for forgiveness say­ ing that in my anger at the children I had forgotten about my vow. Amma then told, “It is not that. If you observe a vow without fasting, no good can come of it.” Again, I asked for forgiveness saying that I did not know the rules to be followed while observing a vow. Amma advised me to observe a vow only from morning till evening and during that time to take only water, milk and coconut juice. If hunger became unbear­ able, fruits were allowed. On requesting Amma to forgive my mistakes and asking her to remove all obstacles, she gave me a mantra. Ever since I started chanting that mantra I am able to observe silence on Saturdays without any hindrance. Three years back in the month of May, on a Saturday at around 11 o’clock in the morning, I was meditating in my pooja room. On seeing a crow pecking and eating a ripe jack fruit my grandaughter came to me asking to pluck it.for her. Coming out of the pooja room, I went to the tree with a hack­ ing knife. As I hacked the stem of the jackfruit which was low enough for me, the knife missed its target, hit the tree and slip­ ping out of my hands hit me a little above my left eye. The eye started bleeding profusely. I covered the wound with my dhoti. Luckily, my nephew who happened to reach there on his cycle, took me to the hospital where the wound was cleaned and bandaged. Still I managed to observe silence. At the same time in Vallickavu, Amma called my son who at that time was residing there and told him “Go home immedi­ ately. Father has met with a minor accident. There is nothing to worry about though, Amma has averted it.” If the wound had been a little to the right, it would have been in the centre of my forehead. If it had been a little below, the knife would have hit my left eye. I came back from the hospital and after sometime my son came rushing in asking “Where is father? What has happened to him?” When we asked him how on earth he came to know about it, he related to us what Mother told him.”208 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Sri R.Reddiar, who resides in Quilon, a town which is about 35 kilometres from Vallickavu, narrates his experience with the Holy Mother as follows: - “A girl who belonged to our
122: family ran away from the house sometime before. As instructed by friends and well- wishers, we went to see the Holv Mother and told her about the girl who was missing. The Mother said, “The girl will be back home seven days from today. You don’t have to go any­ where searching for her.” The Holy Mother gave seven wicks to us and instructed us to light one each day. She continued, “On the seventh day, when you light the last wick, the officials will inform you about the girl’s whereabouts.’ Without being fully convinced by the Mother’s words, we again ran here and there in search of her but got no news at all. We were very distressed at heart especially because of the fact that she was a girl. Greatly disappointed, we again ap­ proached the Holy Mother. She asked us, “Why did you sim­ ply waste so much money? Mother has already told that you will get the girl back. Don't be anxious. You must be firm. As told before, you will get the girl back.” Six days were over and we had burnt six wicks as told by the Holy Mother. On the seventh day, we had lit the last wick dur­ ing the twilight hour. While it was burning in the oil lamp the phone rang. One among the family members attended it. The message was as follow: "Your child is under the custody of the Madras Police. The girl, in order to sell a gold ornament, went to the shop of a goldsmith. Feeling doubtful about her, he informed the police.’ Thus exactly at the correct time as told by the Holy Motherwe got information about the lost girl who was handed over tous soon after. From that time onwards we are living surren­dering everything at the Holy Mother’s Feet. We firmly be­lieve that the Mother will protect us.”Experiences Of Devoiees 209 Kunjamma resides in Kayamkulam about 12 kilometres distant from Mother’s Ashram. The following is an incident which greatly strengthened her devotion to the Holy Mother. A couple of years before coming to the Holy Mother Kunjamma’s husband suddenly died as was foreseen in his horoscope. Thus, Kunjamma developed a strong belief in as­ trology. Leading a miserable life after the death of her hus­ band, she became still more sorrowful when she came to know from her son’s horoscope that he would die at the age of 23 from snake bite. To find a solution and to save her son from this ill fate, Kunjamma went to several places. Finally, she came to the Holy Mother and sought refuge at her feet. The Mother consoled her saying “Don’t worry. Mother will give a mantra to chant daily. Once a week observe a vow for your son. Vows observed by mothers for their children will have more power Your son’s death will get transferred to one of your domestic animals. Go and be peaceful.” Kunjamma was not fully convinced of the Holy Mother's words.
123: Everyday she would see dreams that her son had died from the bite of a snake. After the death of her husband, Kunjamma raised a dog to guard the house. One day, she heard the barking and howling of her pct dog from the front yard of the house followed by the hissing sound of a snake. Full of dread she opened the door a little and peeped out. There she saw the dog and a snake desperately fighting each other. Beholding the fearful sight, she immediately closed the door and sat inside. After a couple of minutes the hue and cry slowly died down. Kunjamma, full of fear, slowly opened the door and much to her amazement found both the dog and snake lying dead. Though she felt a bit dejected about her pct dog's death, she suddenly remembered the Holy Mother’s words,“Your son's death will be transferred to one of your domestic ani­ mals,” and was very grateful to the Mother. This incident hap­ pened exactly during the period which her son was supposed to die according to the horoscope. Kunjamma, full of love and reverence, saluted the Holy Mother mentally. When the 210 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Holy Mother's words came perfectly true Kunjamma’s faith in her also strengthened. Her son is still alive and leading a happy life with the blessings of the Holy Mother. ***** One day in 1980 Chandramati, who resides on the other side of the backwaters in Vallickavu, came to the Holy Mother’s Ashram to receive her blessings. The Holy Mother was receiving the devotees one by one. Suddenly she closed her eyes and sat in an unconscious mood for sometime. Her body became motionless.
