The devotees anxiously waited and within a couple of minutes the Mother came back to her usual state. Calling Chandramati aside, the Holy Mother said, “Your grandchild just died, but the anguished cry of your daughter led Mother there. He came back to life just then.” Chandramati did not fully understand the import of the Holy Mother's words until she came to know the incident which simultaneously occurred in her daughter’s house ex­ actly at the same time. Chandramati’s daughter Nalini lived about 10 kilometres from the Ashram and was also an ardent devotee of the Holy Mother. She had only one son who was then 6 years of age. He suddenly became acutely ill and died on the spot before any medical aid could be provided. There arose an uproar in the house. Nalini, beating her chest and crying loudly supplicated thus, “O Mother, for how long am I calling You and worshipping You sincerely? Is this the fruit of my prayers? Who else is there for me in this world? O Mother, why did You take this my only son, why did you take this only son of mine?" Immediately a flash of lightning was seen near the corpse of Nalini's son. Within a few seconds, the child came back to life as if he was waking up
124: after a deep sleep. Chandramati then related to her daughter what the Holy Mother had told her in the temple and they both then realised that it was only by the Grace of the Holy Mother that the child's life had been re­ stored. Shedding tears of joy, they both reverently saluted the Holy Mother.Experiences Of Devotees 211 The Holy Mother has blessed many childless couples who were unable to bear children for many years by giving them a baby by her divine resolve (sankalpa). Such couples would come and take refuge at the Holy Mother’s feet. Sincere prayer and unshakeable faith in all circumstances are the two most important factors which enable the supplicants to gain the fruit of their life long wish. The refugees also should have self-confidence, mental strength and devotion to confront the trials and tribulations which may occur during the course of pregnancy. The Holy Mother on her part is very strict in choosing the people who really deserve it. This selection of the Holy Mother entirely depends on various subtle consid­ erations which are beyond the realm of human intellect. Even then, there are several devotees who were fortunate enough to receive this great blessing from the Holy Mother. Among them, here we narrate the birth of four such children. Shakti Prasad 1 Vidyadharan and Omana who reside in Vavvakkavu which is 5 kilometres away from Vallickavu were deeply dejected for not having a child even after 9 years of married life. Their par­ enthood hankered to fondle a child. The sweet smile and prattle of a child was just a dream for them which would never come true. It was at this juncture that Sarasamma, Omana’s elder sister and an ardent devotee of the Holy Mother, told her about the Mother. In 1977 Omana came to the Holy Mother’s Ashram accompanied by Sarasamma. When her turn came Omana entered the temple and went near the Mother. Before mentioning anything about her unfortunate life, the Holy Mother consoled Omana saying “Daughter, you came here to gain a baby- Mother will remove your sor­ row. Within four months the child will take form in the womb. " Having full faith in the Holy Mother’s words Omana happily returned home. The Holy Mother’s words came true. Within four months, symptoms of pregnancy appeared in Omana. Vidyadharan, Omana and their relatives were over­212 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI joyed. For 9 years they had been praying and dreaming for a child, and now their life long wish was going to materialise. In those days, Omana was working in a cashewnut factory. When Omana was 4 months pregnant, she was taken to a doc­ tor and it was confirmed that the foetus was alright. In the ninth month she was again examined and was atonished to hear the doctor's report that she was not pregnant! The most surprising thing was her enormous belly which remained unchanged. Omana, her husband and other members of the family were extremely worried. She was taken to
125: another hospital where several gynocologists examined her and found no sign of a baby in the womb. The doctors were as­ tounded. It appeared very strange even to them. A woman with all external symptoms of pregnancy but no trace of a child in the womb. At last one doctor, a specialist in gynocol- ogy, took an X-ray of the mother's womb and was surprised to see dense smoke on the film instead of a foetus. All of them were astonished and at the same time curious to know the explanation. The perplexed relatives took Omana to another hospital in Quilon. All the tests were again repeated. The doctors failed to make a final decision. When there was no sign of a baby in the womb, Omana's relatives became utterly disappointed. Yet Omana, who had unwavering faith in the Holy Mother, was optimistic. One day they came to see the Holy Mother and told her all about the doctors' report and the result of the X-rays. The Mother benignly smiled and said, “Be courageous, that child is God's son, not an image. They cannot take his photograph." Omana perfectly believed the Holy Mother's words. The divine sport did not end there. Omana was ten months pregnant but she didn’t give birth even then. Days and months passed one by one. The situation didn't change. Omana was 13 months pregnant. The villagers abused and teased Omana and the Holy Mother saying “She is going to give birth to a baby elephant?" Some others cuttingly re­ marked, “O, this fishcrgirl has given her a great child; maybe it is a serpent!" Omana and her husband spent their days inExperiences Of Devotees 213 prayer and meditation. Their only succour and support was the Holy Mother. It was really a hard test for them. Sixteen months passed and still Omana had not given birth. The vil­ lagers continued their abuses and sometimes they would even use vulgar expressions. Surrendering everything at the Holy Mother’s feet and observing strict vows, Omana held on to her unswerving faith. Finally, one day during the sixteenth month, the Holy Mother asked Omana's relatives to take her to the hospital. Heeding the Mother's words, Omana was taken to the Victo­ ria Hospital in Quilon. There was a big gathering to hear about this wonderful and extraordinary’ delivery. Everyone anxiously waited for the result. Hearing from Omana’s rela­ tives that she was 16 months pregnant the doctors examined her thoroughly and were amazed to find that there was no baby in the womb even though all indications of pregnancy were physically present. They were in a dilemma. After much discussion, they finally decided on surgery’. When that was over they couldn't believe their eyes; there was a fully grown healthy child in the womb! After being in the mother's womb for 16 months he came out into the world. Named as Sakti Prasad (Blessing of the Supreme Energy) by the Holy Mother, he started to meditate and chant mantras when he
126: was hardly three. Mother gave him a rudraksha rosary and a small seat made of grass. Seated on it, he would meditate sit­ ting in a perfect yogic posture and would repeat the sacred mantras ‘Om Namah Sivaya’ and ‘OM’. Whenever he would come to the Ashram, he would sit in the Holy Mother’s pres­ ence without be prompted by anyone. Picking up some flower petals, he would go on offering them at the Holy Mother’s feet. Often times, the Mother would say, “He has the eyes of a yogi.” He won't speak much. He is not mischie­ vous or naughty like ordinary children. In school he would tell his classmates that “You must meditate on Ammachi’s form or else you will become sick!”214 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Shakti Prasad II Another fortunate couple who received the benedictions of the Holy Mother to have an auspicious child is Ratnamma and her husband Krishnan who reside in Quilon about 35 kilometres from the Mother’s Ashram. Ratnamma came to the Ashram in 1976. There was a particular incident which prompted her to visit the Holy Mother. Rccna, a Christian neighbour of Ratna was afflicted by continuous and agoni­ sing chest pain. Coming to know about the Holy Mother, one day Rccna went to see her. The sacred water and the ash given by the Holy Mother completely relieved Rccna of her chest pain. This experience of her neighbour induced Ratna, who was suffering from acute stomach pain, to seek the blessings of the Holy Mother. She came to Vallickavu and the Holy Mother gave her a banana during the Divine Mood. Ratna ate it and thereby the tormenting stomach pains which had troubled her for so many years stopped. Ratna was married to Krishnan in 1971. Even after five years they had no children. Both of them were extremely sad over their bad luck. Ratna had never mentioned this desire of hers to the Mother. One day the Holy Mother told her, “Is it not your wish to beget a son? Mother will grant it. After 10 months, a son will be born to you.” As per the Holy Mother's words, Ratna became pregnant, even though the statements of the doctors and X-rays proved negative. Taking the doctors' words to be true, Ratna’s hus­ band Krishnan, out of frustration and resentment said, “I won’t allow you to go to Vallickavy until the birth of the child.” During this period in response to Ratna's intense prayers she would often receive visions of the Holy Mother. Exactly on the date predicted by the Holy Mother, a shin­ ing male child was born to Ratnamma. Soon after he started speaking his mother tongue Malayalam, he would say that his real father is God and not Krishnan, his father by blood. Though born and brought up in a fishing village, Sakti Prasad would never eat fish or meat. Without fail he would meditateExperiences Of Devocees 215 every morning and evening. Sometimes he would sit for more than half an
127: hour. On certain occasions, it was even necessary for someone to rouse him from meditation. Even from his infancy, it was the habit of Sakti Prasad io play making temples out of clay and he would worship Lord Siva and God­ dess Parvathi. One day, a stranger came to Sakti Prasad’s house. Rat- namma’s husband Krishnan was out of town. According to the custom, Ratna invited the guest to sit on the verandah and gave him a cup of coffee. All of a sudden, seven year old Sakti came forward and slapped him. Ratnamma immediately interfered and pushed him away. Sakti turned around and told his mother, “Mother, he is a thief. Don't let him sit here, don’t give him coffee.” Ratnamma asked “How do you know that he is a thief” Sakti replied, “By looking at his face.” Hear­ ing this, the thief who came in the guise of the guest, immedi­ ately left the place. Sometimes Sakti Prasad will tell his mother, “You are not my mother. My mother is in Vallickavu,” i.e. the Holy Mother). If anybody in his house or neighbouring houses become sick Sakti will approach them with basil leaves, sacred water and ash. After sprinkling the sacred water on their body, the symptoms of the illness will vanish. Unless his own intuition fells him to do so, Sakti will not do this even if asked. One day, Sakti went to a neighbouring house and ac­ cidently knocked a piece of cake off the table. The girl in that house beat him. The next day, the girl along with her parents came to Sakti’s house. Do you know why? The right arm of the girl with which she had beaten Sakti was swollen and causing her excruciating pain. She couldn’t even move the hand. Pre­ suming that it was a punishment for beating Sakti, they came to seek a solution. Ratnamma suggested to them to go and see the Holy Mother. The next day itself they came to visit the Holy Mother and apologized. After this, the girl’s hand be­ came normal. From his youth itself, Sakti Prasad has been kind and mer­ ciful to others. If somebody in the neighbourhood is starving216 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI for want of food he will bring them to his house and feed them. This nature of his has given a lot of trouble to his family who are financially unstable. If the family is hesitant to give them food, Sakti will feed them with his own share and will fast. Ratnamma considers herself to be Sakti’s foster mother. She raises him on behalf of the Holy Mother and considers the Mother to be his real mother. Unnikannan (Sakti Prasad III) Sarasan and his wife Tankam are from the same island on which the Holy Mother’s Ashram is situated. Although many years had passed after their marriage, they were grief stricken due to not having a child. Having heard from someone about the Holy Mother and her divine powers, they took
128: refuge in her and sincerely prayed for the fulfillment of their hearts desire. One day during her Divine Mood the Holy Mother told Tankam that she would become pregnant soon and within a few days she did indeed become pregnant. One night, when Tankam was three months pregnant, she suddenly had an attack of severe stomach pain followed by continuous bleeding. There were no hospitals or doctors nearby. The family became very distressed. Not knowing what to do, they became distraught. Unable to bear the pain, Tankam cried aloud. At that moment, to everyone’s amazement, the Holy Mother walked into the room. She made some basil leaf extract and asked Tankam to drink it. Within a few seconds her stomach pain and bleeding stopped. How did the Holy Mother come to their house at that time of the night? The Holy Mother along with some other devotees were proceed­ ing to another place on the island in a boat. When the boat reached near Tankam’s house, all of a sudden the Holy Mother's mood changed and she seemed to be very restless. The next moment. Mother seriously insisted that she must get down at Tankam’s house. The devotees in the boat tried to change the Holy Mother’s mind as it was dark and the destina­ tion was still far awav, but Mother was adamant in her deci­Experiences Of Devotees 217 sion and thus the boat was anchored near Tankam’s house. Divine plans are always mysterious. During the course of pregnancy, when Tankam came one day to see the Holy Mother, the later said to her, “You will de­ liver on a dark night and there will be thunder and lightning followed by a heavy downpour.” Exactly as Mother had told, Tankam gave birth to a beautiful male child at the Alleppy Medical College on a gloomy night with heavy rain and thun­ der. The electricity went off in the hospital. Darkness pre­ vailed everywhere in the building But everyone present near the child witnessed something exceptional. It was the shining brilliant eyes of the child piercing the darkness. Named as Unnikannan by the Holy Mother, he grew up as an extraordinary child. He was endowed with a sharp intellect and a face radiating spiritual splendour. Though born and raised in the fisherman clan, he would never eat fish or meat. Sometimes he would ask questions concerning the signifi­ cance of worship and such other religious practices. He would always act according to his own insights. Nobody would scold him or beat him even if he would do some mis­ chief. If by chance he was beaten or scolded, then nobody would be able to sleep that night due to some mysterious dis­ turbances. The Holy Mother once said, “I will give whatever he wants. ” Unnikannan's parents have strong faith in the Holy Mother's assurance. Sakti Prasad IV Hasanu Kunju married Subaida when she was 16 years old. Sixteen more years had passed yet a child was not born to Subaida
129: who was then 32. Her husband, who had undergone many treatments for an infected wound on his shin, went to see the Holy Mother in 1977. When the wound had com­ pletely healed due to the blessings of the Holy Mother, his wife Subaida also had become an ardent devotee of the Mother. Having lost all hope of getting a child, Subaida never opened her heart to the Mother. The doctors were of the opin­218 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI ion that she would be unable to conceive a without undergo­ ing an operation. But one day, understanding Subaida’s heart, the Holy Mother told her, “You are dejected because you haven’t had a child even after so many years. Mother will give a child to you.” Within a short period of time Subaida became pregnant. Thus, Subaida conceived her first child in 1978 at the age of 32 after sixteen years of marriage. This was a remarkable occurance for the villagers and for Subaida’s relatives. Owing to fear of protest and abuse from the Muslim community, Subaida and her husband kept the origin of this pregnancy a secret. In 1979, Subaida gave birth to a resplendant male child. The parents named him Ansim. The neighbours called him Manikantan. The Holy Mother gave him the name Sakti- prasad. He is a phenomenal child endowed with a brilliance and unusual vitality. He attracts the attention of everyone. The Holy Mother alone knows about his bright future. About the birth and future of these children, the Holy Mother says, “During meditation, a power emits from me and enters into the mother’s womb and thus they become preg­ nant. Some of them will become perfect sannyasins. Some others will lead a family life. Wherever they may be, finally they all will turn to spirituality.” Hearing this truth, many of the mothers of these children were feeling sad to think that after some years they would lose their sons for the service of mankind. Therefore, Mother promised them another child which would fulfill all of the expectations that parents usually have for their children, i.e. getting a job and leading a householder's life. Within the next few years, all of these mothers were blessed with another child.Experiences Of Devotees 219 Sarasamma lived with her husband and children in Karunagappally, a small town to the south of Vallickavu. They were leading a happy and contented family life when unexpectedly a great calamity befell them. They lost all their property and even the house in which they were living. They had to live on others liberality and generosity. It was at this juncture that they heard of the Holy Mother. Having taken refuge at her feet, all of the adversities which they had been facing since a long time got resolved one by one. Sarasamma’s faith in the Holy Mother became unshak­ eable. It was Sarasamma’s experience that if a catastrophe were to occur in their house, the Holy Mother would warn them to take necessary care. To get nd of
130: possible misfortunes Mother had given a mantra to chant and instructed her to take a particular vow every week. One day when Sarasamma came to the Ashram the Holy Mother told her, “Daughter, Mother could see a death in your house. Don’t stop your vow. Pray sincerely to God. Don’t worry. Mother is always with you.” Though Sarasamma was very worried, she had strong faith in the Holy Mother's saving grace. Soon the inevitable happened. A couple of days after the Holy Mother had given the warning, Sarasamma’s eldest daughter was bitten by a viper. Immediately they brought the girl to the Mother’s Ashram. By this time, the girl’s whole body had changed its colour to blue. A mucilaginous sub­ stance mixed with saliva started coming from her mouth. Some thought that she was breathing her last and others felt that she was already dead. The devotees tried to console Sarasamma who was sitting near her daughter shedding buckets of tears. The Holy Mother during Devi Bhava sent word that the girl should stay over night in the Ashram. Accordingly she was taken to an­ other building situated on the northern side of the Ashram. The next morning found no change in the girl. It seemed as if there was no sign of life in her. Surrounded by a group of devotees, Sarasamma was still sitting near her daughter.220 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Though she seemed as one utterly collapsed, her strong faith in the Holy Mother enabled her to gather some strength inter­ nally and still be hopeful. All of a sudden, from somewhere a snake appeared in the front yard of the building and straightaway crawled towards the place where the girl was lying. Everyone present there became frightened and called out “Snake, snake! ” Suddenly the girl opened her eyes. By that time, the snake had reached very close to her. Seeing the snake with its hood raised, the girl jolted terribly and got up immediately. To everyone’s astonishment, all the symptoms of snake bite disappeared from the girl's body and she came back to life. The snake imme­ diately crawled away from the spot. Later, Sarasamma and her daughter with overflowing devotion and love prostrated before the Holy Mother and left the Ashram before noon. Owing to the instance of her relatives, Sarasamma at a later date went to see a very famous astrologer. She gave him the necessary’ details about the time of birth, etc. of the concerned person but did not mention that the person was her daughter. Then she asked him about the future of the girl. Having made the astrological calculations, the astrologer said with great conviction, “This particular girl is no more. She had a death caused by poisoning. ” Sarasamma replied, “No, this is my daughter's horoscope and she is still alive. ” The astrologer who had strong faith in his science and ability could not be­ lieve Sarasamma's words. He thought for a moment and again made certain calculations and proclaimed, “Anyhow,
131: I have no doubt that she was bitten by a poisonous snake during that particular time of her predestined death. If she is still alive, then it is a wonder and a rare blessing of God. ” Sarasamma told, “Later we found that even in the horo­ scope written during her childhood, it was mentioned that she would die by poisoning at the age of 19. It was exactly at the same age that she was bitten by the snake. All I can say is that it is the boundless compassion of the Holy Mother that always protects us.”Experiences Of Devotees 221 In 1981, Sarasamma had another unforgettable experi­ ence of Mother’s Grace which she narrated with deep emo­ tion. “In those days, the devotees who came for Mother’s darshan would leave the Ashram around 4 a.m. in order to reach their houses. All the devotees would go in a group up to the bus stand about a half mile away and then take buses to their respective towns. My son Madhu and I boarded the bus and proceeded to our village. It was a New Moon night and although it was 5 o’clock in the morning when we neared the village, it was still pitch dark. I got out at a stop which I thought was just near to my house and presumed that my son also alighted from the bus. Immediately after stepping out of the bus the conductor rang the bell and the bus drove away into the darkness. Looking around, I could not find my son and realised that I had made a mistake. I was at a lonely place about 2 kilometres from my house. In the meantime, my son, on turning around in his seat, was shocked to find me not there. He alighted at the next bus stop and started running back to where I was standing, which was quite a distance away. Perplexed and not knowing what to do. Mother's parting words came to my mind, ‘Be very careful today'. I tightly held Mother's prasad in my right hand. In the meantime, I could see a lorry stopping a short distance away from which seven or eight men emerged and walked towards me. Perhaps they had seen a woman alighting from the bus in this lonely place. Sur­ rounded by these burly looking rowdies, I was shivering with fear as they shot questions one after another at me and spoke using vulgar language. I thought that they may assault me any moment and a terrible fire raged within me. Is this the fate of those who go to see the Holy Mother? Is this the fruit of my life-long devotion? Such thoughts burned within making me oblivious of the surroundings and I shouted out ar the top of my voice ‘yedi!' (O Mother?). This was a great shock to the rowdies who surrounded me.222 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI What followed is difficult to express. Immediately and unexpectedly the effulgent form of the Divine Mother ap­
132: peared before me in the sky with countless arms holding many weapons in Her hands. She was seated on an enormous crea­ ture and Her face, hair and crown exactly resembled those of Mata Amritanandamayi during Devi Bhava. The Holy Mother had assumed the terrible form of Kali in order to save Her devotee’ Seeing this, I started to lose my external aware­ ness. The Divine Mother stretched out Her hands towards me. My eyes became transfixed while gazing at Her radiant form and started to bulge out. My tongue stuck out like Mother Kali’s and I felt a tremendous power pervade my body. An uproarious laughter burst out from within me the mere remembrance of which sends shivers through me. The air trembled with the terrible sound of my laughter. The row­ dies who were about to pounce on me were taken aback on seeing that frightening form with dishevelled hair, bulging eyes, protruding tongue and a roaring laughter standing be­ fore them fearlessly. Perhaps they thought that I was an evil spirit instead of a human being! Losing all of their courage, they now decided to somehow escape and slowly backing up, got into their lorry and drove away posthaste. Even after their departure, I was unable to move. Gradu­ ally, I started to regain my normal consciousness at which time the enchanting form of Mother Kali also disappeared. My body felt numb as if it were paralyzed. After a few minutes, I could move a little and the protruding tongue also withdrew into the mouth. Still I could not move my bulging and trans­ fixed eyes but after massaging them for some time they be­ came normal. My throat pained severely due to the roaring laughter. Looking down, I found that Mother's prasadam was still clasped in my hand. A few more minutes and my son Madhu came running to the spot. ‘Mother, what was that terrible roaring sound? Who were those men driving away in the lorry? Did they do anything to you?’ But I could not answer him as I was still struggling to get back my speech which took some days to return to normal.”Experiences Of Devotees 223 Although this incident took place in 1981, even now when Sarasamma narrates it she shivers with fear, her throat gets choked up with emotion and tears roll down her face while she utters again and again “Amma, Amma, Amma...”. K.S.Gangadharan Vaidyar is a well known and proficient Ayurvedic doctor who lives in Alwaye, a town about 135 kil­ ometres from Vallickavu. Both he and his wife are ardent devotees of the Holy Mother. In 1986, Dr.Gangadharan was suffering from an attack of severe neck pain. Day by day the pain increased until it became unbearable. Dr.Gangadharan says “Though being an experienced Ayurvedic doctor, I was unable to diagnose or cure the severe pain. To reduce the pain a little, I would wear a neck band. Finally, the racking pain became too much to bear and I decided
133: to consult a bone spe­ cialist. The day before the appointment was fixed I visited the Holy Mother’s Ashram. It was a Sunday and was very crowded. When my turn came, I entered the temple where the Holy Mother was seated in Devi Bhava. Having prostrated to the Mother, I hesitantly told her about the acute neck pain which I had been suffering from for a long time and for which I had undergone several treatments. The Holy Mother most affectionately rubbed the afflicted part a couple of times with her left hand and consoled me. Having received the Holy Mother's blessings, both myself and my wife returned home. Finally, we reached home by mid­ night, the excruciating pain persisting. But due to exhaustion caused by the journey I was able to sleep straight away. The next morning when I woke up amazingly enough I found that the pain had gone and to this day it has not returned.” Dr. Gangadharan continued, “For the last 35 years I am working in the medical profession and have undergone dif­ ferent kinds of treatments to cure my neck pain, yet no cure was found. Whereas, the Holy Mother by her mere touch completely removed my pain forever.”224 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Dr. Gangadharan’s sister, Sumathi, was suffering from severe stomach pain. She was 57 years old and a mother of 5 children. Many eminent and renowned doctors were con­ sulted and the prescribed treatments were undergone. None of them were found to be effective. Finally, she was taken to a hospital near Trichur well known for its treatment of cancer. After examining the patient thoroughly the doctors arrived at the conclusion that she was suffering from cancer of the uterus. During this period. Dr. Gangadharan and his wife were travelling in Europe visiting some friends and were unaware of the unfortunate fate of his sister. Upon their return from Europe, they were informed by their relatives of Sumathi’s condition. Dr. Gangadharan narrated, “Hearing about my sister’s pathetic condition myself and my wife rushed to the hospital. Upon seeing, us she burst into tears. After having consoled her we returned home and visited her again after one week. By that time the disease had reached its acute state. Helplessly watching my sister dying in excruciating pain, we could find no way to console her. The doctors were of the opinion that even if the uterus was removed it would be of no use as the disease had advanced too far and her whole systemn was affected. The doctors decided to try to heal the cancer temporarily using radiation and chemotherapy. Soon news spread amongst the relatives that Sumathi was a serious cancer pa­ tient and there was no way to save her life. After having under­ gone the treatments, Sumati was brought to my house for taking complete bed rest. Still the pain remained the same. The racking pain disturbed her continuously which culmi­ nated in insomnia. Even her nature calls were affected due to the
134: disease. All hopes of restoring her health and saving her life were given up. At last, my wife suggested to seek refuge in the Holy Mother who is forever our Stay and Support and who would always stretch forth her blessed hands to protect us from such miseries. Accepting my wife's suggestion, we took SumathiExperiences Of Devotees 225 to the Holy Mother’s Ashram one Sunday, 'Die Holy Mother lovingly caressed Sumathi and rubbing the afflicted part, consoled her. Sumathi felt a great relief by the mere touch of the Holy Mother’s hand. The Holy Mother asked us to bring some tulasi leaf extract during our next visit to the Ashram. The next Darshan itself we brought it as per her instructions. Having blessed the ex­ tract and handing it over to the patient, the Mother asked her to take one teaspoon even’ morning and evening. From the next day onwards, Sumathi followed Mother’s advice. As­ tounding was the outcome; within a week Sumathi’s pain disappeared and her disease was completely cured! Both of these stories are my own personal experiences. There is no greater Guru than one’s own experience. Even though these experiences seem as something great to me they are as nothing for the Holy Mother who is the embodiment of Infinite Power and who can cure the fatal disease of worldly existence itself.” Mr. Gopakumar is the personnel officer in a big fertilizer company at Alwaye. About his experiences with the Holy Mother he tells, “My meeting with the Holy Mother was quite unexpected. It was the day when the devotees had arranged a reception for her in a temple in Ernakulam. The Mother’s devotional singing followed by the reception had a great impact on me. I simply gazed at her loving face and keenly lis­ tened to the poignant devotional songs which revealed the great truths of life. I could not control my tears. When the singing was over, the Mother was receiving the devotees one by one and I heard some people remarking about her spiritual power. ‘She can do all the things which God can do,’ some devotees said. While receiving prasadam from the Holy Mother, I told her about my problem which I felt even God could not solve. ‘Mother, I am a person suffering from tre­ mendous mental depression. For the last 15 years I have been 226 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI suffering from insomnia and cannot sleep even one hour at night. Due to lack of sleep, my face has turned black and dull. I have become very weak as well. Many nights I have sincerely called and prayed to God to give me sleep. All my prayers have ended in vain.’ The Holy Mother smiled at me and affection­ ately smeared some sacred ash on my forehead. She did not even utter a word. I returned home when Mother left the place. It was midnight when I had my supper after which I felt very tired. It was 12:30, but as sleep was beyond
135: my imagina­ tion, I simply hid down on the cot. I opened my eyes when I heard my servants calling me, “Sir, please get up. What kind of sleep is this?” I was shocked when the servant boy told me that it was 9 o’clock in the morning. Fortunately, it was a Sunday and I didn’t have to go to work, The boy served me a glass of tea, yet I was unable to keep my eyes open. Effortlessly I again went back to sleep. When I opened my eyes it was 1 o’clock in the afternoon. Getting up from bed, I came to the dining room and saw both my breakfast and lunch sitting there on the table. It was like a dream. It took me a couple of minutes to convince myself of this miracle. From that day until today, I am getting good sleep. It is all due to the Mother’s blessings, and her boundless compassion which has given me new vigour and strength. Soon I visited the Holy Mother at her Ashram. Still I had a lot of family problems. During my first visit to the Ashram I did not tell any of this to the Holy Mother. Anyhow, after seeing the Holy Mother during Devi Bhava, I felt that my dif­ ficulties were getting less and less. Three years ago, due to my father-in-law’s behaving rudely, I chided him severely. As a protest against this, he pressed his daughter, who was a bank employee, to get a transfer to Trivandrum where he lived. Owing to her father’s persistance , my wife was forced to obey him. Thus we were separated. My only son also went with my wife. I was therefore leading a miserable life steeped in the sorrow of separation. My wife’s family was trying to cut off our relationship by slowly turning my wife and son against Experiences Of Devorees 227 me. During my second visit I prayed to the Holy Mother to save me. She smilingly consoled me saying, ‘Son, don’t worry. Mother will look after it.’ Though Mother said these words, I wasn’t fully convinced. The next morning when I was attending my job I felt an unusual mental calmness and delight. I could discharge all my duties sincerely and easily. At noon, I received a telegram containing the following message: ‘Balamurali seriously ill, admitted in hospital...Ammini.’ This was from my wife con­ cerning my son. Even after reading the message I did not feel any anxiety’ or sadness at all. Immediately I left work and went to tell the news to one among the Mother's devotees. When I reached his house another devotee of the Mother was also present. I still remember their words, ‘Gopakumar, don’t worn’, this is just Mother's technique to rejoin both of you. Your son’s illness is just to make you go there.' I reached Trivandrum at 10 o'clock at night and went straight to the hospital where my son was. When I entered the room where
136: he was lying he suddenly jumped up calling out, ‘Mother, my father has come.' He seemed to be alright. Then my wife briefly told everything which had happened. She said, ‘In the morning his temperature was 105 degrees. All of us were very worried. But later on in the evening the tempera­ ture subsided and he became normal.' Then I remembered the words of my friends and I bowed my head at the Holy Mother's feet for showering so much of Grace upon me. I happily spent two days with my wife and son in the hospital and on the third day I returned to Emakulam after getting my son discharged. The following Sunday as usual, I went to Vallickavu to visit the Holy Mother during Devi Bhava. As it was crowded that day I didn't get a chance to tell her about my meeting with my wife and son. Anyhow, I thought, she knows everything. She would say, ‘Mother knows the problems of her children without being told. She can see their aching heart? As usual, the Holy Mother gave me an incense stick and sacred ash as prasadam and said, ‘Son, when you reach home all your sor­228 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI rows will be removed? That same night I returned home by 1 o’clock. When I entered my bedroom I was surprised and at the same time overwhelmed with joy to see both my wife and son lying fast asleep on my bed. When I saw my wife and son back home who had been separated from me for the last three years, I stood in front of the portrait of the Holy Mother and with joined palms, having lit some incense, I prayed, ‘My dear Mother, you can do those things which even God cannot do! ’ K.K. Damodaran Nair was a relative of one of the Holy Mother’s devotees who was for some months staying in his house in Bombay while employed there. Often Mr. Nair would see his relative sitting before the photo of a lady wor­ shipping and crying and singing bhajans. He would think, 'What a waste of time, to cry to a photo. Who is this so called Mother?' He was very skeptical but never expressed it nor interfered with his relative's worship. During this period, Mr. Nair returned for a visit to his family house which was about 170 kilometres from the Holy Mother's Ashram. Upon arriving there, he found his 80 year old father working in the garden. He said to him, "Father, you are so old, why should you be doing such strenuous work?” His father replied, "From youth itself I am accustomed to doing hard work. I don't feel any strain.” Mr. Nair then vis­ ited the house of his relative who was staying with him in Bombay. That man's father was also a staunch devotee of the Holy Mother. The two of them decided to pay a visit to the Holy Mother, Mr. Nair more out of curiosity than
137: anything else. When they arrived it was Krishna Bhava. Entering the temple, Mr. Nair was astounded to see the shining face and mischievous smile of Krishna. While standing before Krishna during Darshan, he suddenly started dancing. He could not control himself. Krishna then asked him, "Now do you believe?” He felt humiliated and very confused but stillExperiences Of Devotees 229 was not ready to fully believe in the Holy Mother. He replied, “Give me an experience and then I will believe.” With a smile on her face. Mother said, “You will get one.” After returning to Bombay, Mr. Nair was working a night shift one night at around midnight when he fell asleep on his desk. Suddenly at 2 a.m., he felt someone tapping him on the shoulder. Startled, he woke up to see the Holy Mother before him saying “Did you know that your father has died? Look and see.” Suddenly he saw the corpse of his father, lamps lit both at his head and feet and surrounded by crying relatives. The Mother then asked him, “Now do you believe?” Mr. Nair did not know what to think of this experience. How could it have been a dream when he had seen the Mother standing be­ fore him with his own eyes? Then again, how could it have been real since only a few days ago he had seen his father as strong and healthy as ever working in the garden? After completion of his work, he returned home and re­ lated the incident to his wife. She told him, “Don’t pay any attention to a silly dream. It must be only a figment of your imagination. Go to sleep.” A couple of hours later a telegram arrived telling, ‘Father expired at 1 a.m. Start immediately.’ After this incident, Mr. Nair gained full faith in the Holy Mother's divinity and became an ardent devotee. He now worships the photo of the Holy Mother like his relative used to do. Puvampuzha Swami was well known in his village near Quilon. When he heard about the Holy Mother and her Di­ vine Moods as Krishna and Devi, the Swami decided to go and test her spiritual power. After his first visit to the Ashram in 1976, he could not return to meet the Holy Mother for some time due to the death of his mother. During his second visit the Swami thought, ‘If she really has the power of Krishna, let her tell me any one of the doctrines of the Bhagavad Gita. Then only I will believe that she is genuine.’230 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI The Swami entered the temple with the aforesaid sankalpa in his heart when the Holy Mother was in Krishna Bhava. He told her, “Owing to my mother’s death, I have been unable to come here for the last so many days.” The Holy Mother re­ torted, “That which is not, never comes into being and that which is, never ceases to exist. Forget not this great truth.” Hearing
138: this, the Swami became full of awe and reverence. This particular saying of the Holy Mother, though not learnt from any of the scriptures, was an exact translation of the 16th verse of Chapter 2 of the Bhagavad Gita. Hearing this, the Swami became fully convinced of the Holy Mother’s greatness and became an ardent devotee of hers. Radha Bai, who hails from Calicut, was an ardent devotee of Sri Satya Sai Baba. On the 23rd of January, 1985 at night she had a dream. Sri Satya Sai Baba along with another holy woman dressed in pure white clothes appeared to her. Both of them were standing in front of a big house. Pointing to the holy woman, Baba told her “You must go and see this Mother.” Having said this, both of them disappeared with­ out another word. Radha Bai, whose sleep was now dis­ turbed, got up and thought, ‘Where shall I go to meet this Mother? How can I know the place where she is staying?’ In the newspaper the next day, Radha Bai found a write up and picture of a holy woman announcing her arrival in Calicut that very same day. She could easily recognize her face. It was the Mother whom she saw with Baba in the dream. The place and the house where she would stay during her visit was also mentioned in the article. Radha Bai was overwhelmed with joy. That day itself Radha Bai and her family went to meet the Holy Mother. When they arrived at the house where the Holy Mother was staying, Radha Bai was even more amazed be­ cause it was the very same house which she had seen the previ­ ous night in her dream. Everyone had darshan of the HolyExperiences Of Devotees 231 Mother. While taking darshan, Radha Bai related the story’ of her dream to the Holy Mother. Mother quietly listened and then gently smiled as if to say that she was already aware of the fact! Radha Bai and family returned home feeling highly ele­ vated and blessed. During the Holy Mother’s first visit to Calicut, many devotees had the good fortune to have several inspiring spiri­ tual experiences. Mrs. Kunjukutty Amma, whose Chosen Deity was the Divine Mother, was worshipping Devi as Moo- kambika (an aspect of the Divine Mother) for the last so many years. Along with her family, she came to see the Holy Mother on the 24th of January, 1985. The following is her experience with the Holy Mother in her own words. “When I approached the Holy Mother * her penetrating glance fell on me. The very’ first look itself made all my hairs stand on end. I even felt that I was losing my body-conscious­ ness. I prostrated to the Mother and supplicated loudly, ‘O Mother! Great Enchantress? Mookambika! Protect me, O Mother? Give refuge, give refuge! ’ Most affectionately the Holy Mother placed my head on her lap. Slowly raising my head from her lap, the Mother
139: applied sacred ash on my fore­ head between the eyebrows with her left forefinger. As soon as the Mother touched my forehead, I experienced a terrific jolt all over the body like an electric shock. The next moment, I clearly saw my beloved Deity, Mookambika sitting in the lotus posture adorned with divine ornaments where the Holy Mother was seated. ” Kunjukutty Amma, in a God-intoxicated mood, started dancing and singing. The Holy Mother asked “Child, what do you want?” In the same rapturous mood she replied, “Blessed am I for having touched Thy Lotus Feet. I have seen Parashakti (the Supreme Energy)! O Mother, please do not go away abandoning me.”MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI 232 She continued dancing blissfully and shed tears of joy. Preventing some devotees who tried to carry her out of the room, the Holy Mother said, “Nobody should touch her. She is enjoying bliss.” This blissful mood of Kunjukutty Amma’s continued for half an hour. Having seen her beloved Deity’s beautiful form, she returned home with an overflowing heart. Kunju Raman, a high school teacher, gives the following account of his experience with the Holy Mother: “Having been initiated into the Siddha Vidya (a type of spiritual practice) by His Holiness Swami Sivananda Par- amahamsa, I have been following the instructions of my guru as best as a householder with his limitations can. I have been doing this for the past twelve years. Fully conscious of the established fact that this Siddha Vidya and scriptural texts like Bhagavad Geeta can take me to my Supreme Goal, I de­ cided not to go to anyone else for guidance. But in 1985, a lecturer friend and neighbour of mine told me of his experiences at Calicut with a Holy Mother Mata Amritanandamayi. He told me that he had spiritual experi­ ences when Mother merciy touched him. He also showed me a booklet containing Mother's sayings and I also had a chance to listen to Mother's bhajan, 'Manase Nin Svanthamayi' on a cassette. Even though I was not in good health, I decided that I would take any amount of trouble and get a darshan of this Holy Mother. I discussed this matter with a dose friend and yoga practitioner, Sri K.S.Nair. He also agreed to accom­ pany me. Thus we reached that holy place on 20 October, 1985 at 4 in the evening. Amma was giving darshan in the hut. When my turn came to meet Amma, she kindly enquired my name and place and said that we would meet again during Bhava Darshan. During the Darshan, Amma forcefully pressed the centre part of my forehead and asked me to sit by her side and meditate.Experiences Of Devotees 233 The next day, while Sri Nair and myself were standing in front of the shrine totally at a loss at not being able to find a solitary place to meditate, Amma suddenly appeared in front of us. Saying, “Come, sons’” Amma touched us and took us to the meditation hall on
140: the southern side where some of the brahmacharis were meditating. Amma made us sit down and started meditating with us. This incident really surprised us. Amma, who was somewhere in the Ashram, suddenly ap­ peared in front of us knowing our desire to meditate. This incident revealed her high state to us. We believe that it is this experience of ours that later was responsible for creating a group of devotees in Ambalapara. In November and December, my nose was bleeding from time to time. Even after consulting a doctor and undergoing treatment continuously, the bleeding continued to trouble me. We came to know that Amma was being received at Sri Ramakrishna Ashram, Pallapuram on the 4th of January and so my friend Sri Narayanan Nair and myself decided to go and have her darshan as well as to finalise the details for the pro­ gramme to be held in Ambalapara on the 17th. It was only after having Mother's darshan that we realised that wc had forgotten to mention about my bleeding nose problem. Taking all the medicines, I threw them out saying “If it is the Will of Amma, the disease will leave me of its own accord.” Strange but true, I saw the last of that disease and have not had that problem to this day. When Amma visited Ambalapara in 1986, we had the good fortune of welcoming Amma to our humble abode. During the pada pooja (worship offered to Amma’s holy feet) the imprint of the holy feet could be clearly seen on the plate in which they were washed. That plate which bears the imprint of Amma’s holy feet is kept nowadays in our meditation room and worshipped by us as well as by Amma’s devotees. This imprint can still be seen clearly even today.”234 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Seshan, a devotee hailing from Madras, was an electrical engineer. After retirement he dedicated his whole time to organising spiritual discourses and serving sannyasins. Even though he did quite a lot of spiritual practice, no progress was made until he met the Holy Mother in 1984. After his first meeting with the Holy Mother, Seshan several times prayed to her to initiate him with a mantra. For some reason or other. Mother did not pay much attention to his request. Neverthe­ less, Seshan persisted in his request. It was during this period that the Holy Mother visited Tiruvannamalai, the abode of Ramana Maharshi. Seshan with his family came to visit the Mother there. This time, when Seshan expressed his cher­ ished desire to the Mother, immediately she told him that she would initiate him during his next visit to Vallickavu. As the Holy Mother had promised, Seshan received his initiation when he next visited the Ashram. Though a Darshan day, it was not very crowded. At eleven in the night, just before the end of Devi Bhava, Seshan was initiated by the Holy Mother. Before leaving the temple she instructed the devotees, “This son should neither be touched
141: nor be called by anyone.” Soon after the initiation, Seshan became immersed in meditation. After three hours had passed, he was still sitting in the temple unmoved. Adhering to the Holy Mother’s in­ structions, two of the residents of the Ashram guarded the temple doors to avoid any possible disturbances. Just after two in the morning, Seshan opened his eyes and looked around perplexed. He asked one of the residents, “Where is everybody? Where is Mother? Why is there no bhajan? I have meditated only for a few minutes, but what has happened?” The resident exclaimed, “A few minutes! Three hours have passed. Mother initiated you at 11 o’clock and now it is 2 o’clock. The Bhava Darshan finished a long time ago.” Hearing the words of the resident, Seshan’s amazement and happiness knew no bounds. “The Mother’s Grace!” he exclaimed. The resident’s curiosity was aroused and he asked, “If you don’t mind, we are interested to know what experi-Experiences Of Devotees 235 ence you had. As spiritual practitioners, it would be an inspi­ ration for us.” Seshan responded with delight, “How can I express it through words? After imparting the mantra, the Holy Mother touched the spot between my eyebrows with her fore­ finger. The impact of that touch was wonderful. I felt some­ thing like a very strong electric shock permeating my whole body. The temple and the entire world revolved at high speed until it disappeared from my sight. After this, what I could feel was only bliss. You told me that I sat for three hours in meditation, but it felt as if only three minutes had passed. What a wonder, there was neither space nor time!” After hearing this story, the resident’s curiosity only in­ creased. With a little bit of hesitation he asked, “May I know the mantra which was given to you? If you think it proper, please do tell me. I am inspired to ask this because from your explanation it is clear that you were transported to a formless state soon after the initiation.” Seshan replied, "A sadhak is not supposed to reveal these things to anybody else. It should be kept as a secret. But I can tell you this much. I am a Vedantin, a worshipper of the form­ less Supreme Consciousness. For many years now I have been chanting a mantra, but without having had any experience. The Holy Mother initiated me with the same mantra. Not only did she give the mantra to me, but she blessed me by be­ stowing the experience of it as well.” Mr. P.O. Narayan Nambiar who hailed from Calicut, one of the three major cities in Kerala, narrates his experience as follows: "Bring a devotee of Lord Krishna from a very young age, I always used to chant His Divine Names and sing devo­ tional songs about Him. At the age of 17, I was initiated by the disciple of a Great Soul, Sivananda Paramahamsa. From that time onwards, besides singing the
142: glories of the Lord I also used to practice meditation.236 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI “It was in the year 1960 when the following incident oc- cured. One night after my usual singing and meditation, I went to bed. At 3.30 a.m. I had a dream in which I saw a big hall with a courtyard in front of it. In the courtyard there were different kinds of colourful plants, both in flower pots and on the ground. There I saw two figures; one was that of a holy man wearing a bright orange gown, with a mop of tight curly hair. The other was a holy woman dressed in white who made a sign with her right hand beckoning me to follow them. The holy man neither looked at me nor gestured. Though only a dream, I was firmly convinced that there must be some signifi­ cance behind it. The dream and the Great Souls who I saw in it have remained ever vivid in my mind. “It was not until two years later, when he was first becom­ ing known in our area, that I discovered that the holy man was Sri Satya Sai Baba of Puttaparthi. Another twenty years passed before on the 26th of January’, 1985 I realized that the holy woman whom I saw in the dream was Mata Amritanan­ damayi. That is when I saw her for the first time in the home of a devotee in Calicut. “When I met the Mother, I requested her with all humility, I have been practicing meditation since the age of 17. But until now I have not experienced the state of samadhi. Please bless me by bestowing that boon on me? The Mother looked in my eyes and said, ‘Son, intensify your sadhana and pro­ ceed? “Having received her blessings while I was standing out­ side the house, I saw the Holy Mother with some of her dis­ ciples getting ready to depart in order ^o participate in a recep­ tion followed by a consecration ceremony in a temple. I stood a short distance away from the entrance gate thinking to de­ part only after the car carrying the Holy Mother had left the compound. She was about to step into the car when suddenly the Holy Mother noticed me standing a short distance away. Immediately she withdrew her step and walked towards me. Joining my palms, I bowed down to her. She touched the back of my palms with her finger and said, ‘Son, Mother has agreedExperiences Of Devotees 237 to go, but she will return soon.’ That touch of the Holy Mother was like a tremendous electric shock. It shook me and my whole body was jolted from head to toe. My eyes automati­ cally closed and filled with tears. Four or five seconds passed, and when I opened my eyes again Mother had already entered the car and was departing. I stood transfixed on the same spot until Mother returned. The touch of the Holy Mother had
143: a great impact on me. My spiritual practices have strengthened and my concentration has increased. Above all, my craving for worldly pleasures which existed until the previous day mysteriously and effortlessly disappeared from my mind. This state of mind continues even today. “In April 1985, while attending a 7 day spiritual camp at the Holy Mother’s Ashram I asked her, ‘Mother, I have read in some spiritual texts that if one could find the Spiritual Master of his previous birth then he could quickly gain spiritual prog­ ress. Mother, were you my Guru in a previous birth?’ Then the Holy Mother smilingly said, ‘Yes, son.’ I humbly requested, ‘Mother, if so, kindly initiate me.' The Holy Mother affec­ tionately replied, ‘During your next visit Mother will initiate you.’ As told by the Holy Mother, during my next visit to the Ashram on the 12th of May 1985, she initiated me at the end of Devi Bhava. Having been initiated, the Holy Mother put a trident in my hands and instructed me to meditate as much time as I wanted to. From the moment she handed over the trident to me until I placed it back in its former place, the tri­ dent strongly vibrated as if there was electricity in it. In order to convince myself that it was not a mere hallucination of my mind, several times I released one hand and held it with the other. But the vibration did not stop; it was real. Even my hands shook in consonance with the vibrations of the trident. This continued for one and a half hours until I returned the trident to its original place. Now I was convinced that the Holy Mother was a Perfect Master and that she could transmit her spiritual energy’ even to an inert object. These experiences and many others strengthened my faith in the Holy Mother and now I have dedicated my whole life at her Lotus Feet.238 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI Experiences of Spiritual Aspirants Unnikrishna was the first person who was fortunate enough to meet Mother and stay with her for a long time. He did not continue his schooling after the sixth grade. In him we see a wonderful example of the Mother’s kindness and grace. If the Guru’s Grace is there, even an illiterate layman can be­ come an immortal poet. Unnikrishna’s life is the best testi­ mony to this. After completing his brief schooling, the young Unni wandered freely engaging himself in a variety of activities. In 1976, when he was twenty years old, he heard about the Holy Mother and came to see her. He felt great faith and devotion for Amma from that very first meeting. Thereafter, he fre­ quently visited her and sought her instructions. A year passed in this way when one day. Mother asked him to stay with her in order to perform the daily worship in the temple. She in­ structed him to chant the Lalita Sahasranama (The Thousand Names of the Divine Mother) every
144: day. From that point onwards there came a complete revolu­ tion in his life. The mere presence of the Holy Mother inspired him with a strong desire to realize the Truth. His days were filled with the practice of austere penance, formal worship, conversations with Amma, reading of scriptures, and other spiritual activities. As a result of this disciplined way of life, he gradually realised that the glorious Mother of the Bhava Darshan, and the everyday Mother in her more usual moods were in fact one and the same, two facets or manifestations of the same infinite Divine Power played Out for the good of the world. This insight greatly stimulated his desire to perform sadhana (spiritual practices) and he surrendered himself fully at Mother’s feet regarding her as his sole support in life. As time passed, his spiritual disciplines took an even more rigorous turn; he came to eat, sleep and speak less. On occa­ sion, he fasted for several weeks at a stretch. He used to sleep on the bare ground without so much as a blanket to cover himself during the winter and the rainy season. When he wentExperiences Of Devotees 239 on pilgrimage, as he occasionally did, he travelled on foot the whole way without recourse to any other form of convey­ ance. One day, with tears in his eyes, he asked Mother with over­ flowing emotion, “Who is my real Mother?” Regarding him with great tenderness, the Holy Mother took his head on her lap and replied, "My child, you are my son and I am yourmother.” Unnikrishna was overwhelmed by an inexpressiblebliss welling up from within. As he silently gazed at Mother’sradiant face, he shed tears of joy. By the infinite Grace of the Mother, Unnikrishna has be­ come a prolific poet whose works are pregnant with deepphilosophical import and devotional sweetness. Once, whenhis parents had sent some relatives to bring him back home, hereplied with the following poignant verse: Having left my home long ago If I were to now lead a worldly life would I gain Peace of mind thereby? What profit has there been in such an existence Even from time immemorial? When l am struggling to free myself From the utter folly of the world, Why do you pave a fool's path straight To the servitude of the beggar? Can I ever agree to such a fate? Unni describes his first meeting with Mother in the fol­ lowing evocative way: In a distant temple a glowing flame burnt Without getting extinguished. The Mother with infinite compassion was Seated there as a guiding light far those Wandering destitute in the darkness. One day while I wandered there240 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI That embodiment of mercy called me aside. Opening the inner sanctum She dabbed sandalwood paste on my forehead. Melodiously singing the exalted praises of the Lord, She made a place for me on Her soft and Sacred arm. Coming near, a wondrous divine Dream whispered this truth in my ear: “What need is
145: there for tears? Don't you know That you have come near the Mother of the Universe?” With a sigh I awoke, Her Lotus Face Indelibly impressed on my consciousness. On one occasion, due to some inner conflict, Unnikrishna fasted for several weeks. When the Holy Mother came to know about it, she also stopped eating and drinking. Unni, unaware of her fasting, continued his vow. After a couple of days, while doing his daily worship, the Holy Mother's father scolded him severely for not taking food and thereby making Mother starve. Soon after the worship was over, he came to the doorway of Mother's hut with a heavy heart and eyes filled with tears. Calling him to her side, she caressed him with great love and looking at his shivering body said, “Unni, my son, if there is some inner agitation you should come and tell Mother about it. Don't torture your body like this. To do tapas, the body is needed. Eat at least for the sustenance of the body.” Saying this, she asked for a plate of rice and fed Unni with her own hands, herself eating out of the same plate. A few months after his settling in the Ashram, Unni, whose nature was that of a wandering mendicant, decided to go away. Without informing anyone, he made ready for his jour­ ney. It was a Darshan night and as he was about to depart, a man suddenly came sent by the Mother and said, “Mother says that even though you are getting ready to go, you should not depart now.” Unable to disobey the Holy Mother, he cancelled his journey. Once more he tried to go at a later date but the same incident repeated itself. Finally, he did actuallyExperiences Of Devotees 241 leave the place but had to return after two days. He thereby became convinced that without Mother’s knowledge and blessings, nothing can be done. Mother once told that “Unni’s songs spring from his medi­ tation.” What greater recognition could there be beyond this? The following is a translation of two of his songs: I have wandered in distant lands carrying a Heavy burden of sorrow. At last, coming near You I have surrendered myself at Your Lotus Feet. O Mother, won't You kindly wash away my Endless afflictions and tears with the waters of Your Love? Consider not this poor one as a sinner as no one Else is there in tins world as my stay and support. O Embodiment of Compassion, please carress Me with the Moonlight of Your beautiful eyes. ***** O Mother, throwing away this heavy burden of Thoughts let me sit near You and merge in Meditation. O the One who is described in the Vedas And Vedanta! O the Mother of all the Gods and Goddesses! Won't You fulfill this wish springing from my Soul to gain the Supreme Self? O Mother, when Will that time come when I will renounce all Taste for pleasure-seeking and become one with
146: Your Holy Feet? ***** Born and brought up in an orthodox brahmana family, Chandru (Adhyatma Chaitanya) had immense faith in the Divine Mother even from his childhood. He would confide all 242 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI of his problems to Her while visiting a small Devi temple near his house. Having failed in his exams for the Bachelor of Arts Degree in 1975, he left his home for Madras in great disap­ pointment and stayed with his uncle there. One day, his uncle accosted him telling him, “Don’t spend your life simply sit­ ting idle. Try to get a job.” Taking his advice to heart, Chandru searched for employment and finally got a job work­ ing in a fishing boat. From then onwards, he began to experi­ ence both the ups and downs of life. At this time, Chandru became closely associated with a Hindu monk who was engaged in giving classes in Hindu scriptures. Thenceforth, he dedicated most of his time to spiritual activities like scriptural study, devotional singing, etc., and simultaneously attended to his official work in the fishing boat. One day, Chandru suddenly felt that he must go back to his home in Kerala. Within a couple of days, he left Madras and reached Alleppey but found it extremely difficult to live with his relatives who insisted that he appear again for the B.A. exams. It was at this time that he came to hear about the Holy Mother and accompanied by a friend, he reached her Ashram one night around 10 o’clock in 1978. When Chandru saw the Holy Mother emerge from the temple transformed into the Universal Mother during the Devi Bhava, he felt that he was standing before the Divine Mother Kali of Dakshinesvar. After her ecstatic and blissful dance, the Holy Mother seated herself inside the temple and Chandru entered to have her Darshan. When the Holy Mother pressed her forefinger between Chandru’s eye­ brows, he had the vision of Kali Mata as seen in the Bhavatar- ini Temple in Calcutta. Mother asked him to sit and meditate and he soon became immersed in meditation for more than two hours. While witnessing the Holy Mother sucking the blood and pus out of the stinking wounds of Dattan the leper, Chandru’s heart overflowed with love and reverance for her. This was proof enough for him to understand and believe in the Holy Mother’s incomparable greatness. At that momentExperiences Of Devotees 243 he decided to renounce worldly life and embrace the life of spirituality. The next morning while taking leave of the Holy Mother, Chandru asked, “May, I go. Mother?” replying which she told, “Son, nobody can go anywhere. Where to go?” Unable to understand the Vedantic import of her words, Chandru left the Ashram. Soon after his departure, the Holy Mother turned to a close devotee and said, “He is my son. He will re­ turn immediately.” In the meantime, Chandru, who had reached the other side of the river, suddenly had an intense urge to go
147: back to the Ashram and so he returned on the very same ferry' that had taken him across. Mother, with a loving smile said, “Consider the whole world as your Guru. Don’t think that Mother is this body. The body is inert. Don’t you see how an infected wound on the body begins to stink? How can the body be one’s real Self? You have studied so many things but nothing has en­ tered into your heart. You have no devotion.” Hearing Mother’s words, Chandru wept like a child. He left the Ashram as an utterly changed person endowed with a resolute mind bent on following the spiritual path. With his mind fixed on the Holy Mother’s divine form, he reached home and relinquishing his luxurious ways of living, he went without food and sleep for three day’s and nights. Telling his parents that he was going to observe a special vow in the presence of the Holy Mother in order to remove the influence of evil forces on himself, Chandru came for a three days stay at the Ashram. When the Holy Mother saw him, she told him, “You have come here after telling a lie to your par­ ents. It is alright. It is to know the Truth that you have done so.” Soon after, Chandra's parents also came to visit the Holy Mother and became her ardent devotees. Chandru had an intense desire to become a monk. The Holy Mother told him, “If your mother in Alleppey agrees, then only will I accept you.” Before coming to the Mother, Chandru had been very hot tempered by nature. His sudden and surprising change was something unbelievable to his244 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI parents and other relatives. They were very’ happy about it, especially his mother. Being a pious and devoted lady, she readily consented to Chandru’s desire to become a monk and offered her son at the Holy Mother’s Feet. On that auspicious occasion, Chandru’s mother asked the Holy Mother, “O Mother, how did you tame this Chandru who was so hot- tempered?” The Mother smilingly replied, “Don’t you know that he has come to Bhadrakali (the fierce aspect of the Divine Mother)?” Because of the lack of facilities to stay at Vallickavu, Chan­ dru requested Mother to send him somewhere to do his spiri­ tual practices. The Holy Mother thereupon sent him to Tiruvannamalai (where resided the famous sage Sri Ramana Maharshi) in order to perform 41 days of intense spiritual discipline. Chandru reached Tiruvannamalai on 11 October, 1979 where he met Nealu, Gayatri, Ganga (Sarvatma Chaitanya) and Madhu (Prematma Chaitanya). Later on, all of them became Mother’s disciples and settled in her Ashram as permanent residents. During his stay in Tiruvannamalai, Chandru wrote the fol­ lowing song: O Mother who dwells in the heart O Mother who is the embodiment of love, I knew nothing but to utter Your Divine Names. O Mother of the Universe, bless me To transmit Your divine message. I want not Worldly enjoyments but
148: only pure devotion to You. How many births have passed in vain while I Remained unable to merge in Your Sacred Feet. O Mother, only because of Your immense Compassion I have come to You now in this birth. O Ocean of Pure Love, I offer my self to You. I want nothing, not even life itself, without Knowing You.Experiences Of Devotees 245 Carrying these burdens, I have passedThrough many births without knowing ou.In this birth, having come to Tote, I haveGiven all burdens to You. O Mother, I cannot See anything permanent except for YourInfinite Power. Merging In that Stream ofConsciousness, let the I’ in me disappear. Though Mother told me that I and She are notSeparate, at present I have not the power to see usAs one and so wish to remain before Her as HerChild. Mother always raises Her children withGreat care and love and by the mere touch of HerSacred hands all of my sins will be washed away. O Mother, why are You so late?Is not this child Your own? Thinking that I amYour own child, I am spending my daysCounting the moments. O Mother, what shall Ido to merge in You? Won't You please show methe correct path? O Mother, I am nothing. Youare everything, you Are everything... After returning from Tiruvannamalai, Chandru spent 2months in the holy Presenceof the Mother during which timehe underwentstrict and severe discipline under her guidance.After 2 months, in obedience to Mother’sinstructions, he went in the middle of March,1980 to Sandheepany Sadha-nalaya inBombay, a part of the Central ChinmayaMissionTrust, in order to study the Hinduscriptures. The day before his departureMother told him, “Have faith in your Guru,God and the Scriptures. The intention of yourstudies shouldbe for the good of the world. Don’t listen to those who abuseyour Guru.” During his stay in Bombay, Chandru had several visions of Mother scolding him for not doing his spiritual practices properly. Thus, the Holy Mother, though physically thou­ sands of miles away in a remote village, would control and246 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI discipline Chandru studying in Bombay. She would send let­ ters to him containing spiritual instructions and philosophi­ cal statements presented in her own simple and lucid style. These letters were of great help to him in moulding his charac­ ter and integrating his personality. Having completed his studies, Chandru, now known as Adhyatma Chaitanya, returned from Bombay to Vallickavu in March, 1983 and worked for the Chinmaya Mission Trust for a year. At the same time, the Holy Mother asked him to give classes for the residents of the Ashram. On 24 December, 1984 he resigned from the activities of the Chinmaya Mission and dedicated all his time to serving the Holy Mother and spreading her divine message. ***** Balu (Amritatma Chaitanya) narrates the following expe­ riences of Mother’s Grace: “When my B.A. final examinations were over, I heard about a girl endowed with supernatural powers who would appear as Devi and Krishna. Though my faith in the existence of God was
149: deep-rooted, I was not much interested in seeing her. Some of my relatives and friends who had already visited her place spoke very highly of her and continued to press me to visit her Ashram. Finally, one evening with a skeptical mind I came to the Ashram accompanied by my uncle. It was already dark and as I approached the Ashram the strains of a heart­ capturing devotional song reverberated in my cars. It ar­ rested my attention. I entered into the Ashram premises where there was a small shrine in which I saw a girl in white clothes singing songs laden with love and devotion. Listen­ ing to the song, one could see that her heart was overflowing in divine bliss and love. The vibrations created by the song pierced my heart and evoked my tender feelings. “When my turn came, I entered into the shrine room where she was seated on a pcetham or stool. I prostrated before her and when I got up, she took hold of my hand and looked intoExperiences Of Devotees 247 my eyes. Her eyes were beaming like the full moon. That look penetrated through me, that smile bound me and made me motionless. Compassion breaking all its limits manifested on her face. Slowly she put my head on her shoulder and softly but emphatically said, ‘Child, I am your mother and you are my child.’ That sweet voice entered deep into my heart and I became enraptured in an inexplicable joy. This is what I had been searching for. I burst into tears. Love in all its purity— Motherhood on a universal scale had assumed a form. Thrilled by this first experience, I sat near Mother the whole night. “When I reached home the next day, I realised the great change which had taken place in me. I became totally indiffer­ ent to all my usual activities. My desire to see her again inten­ sified. All my thoughts were fixed on her. That night I was unable to sleep. Whenever I tried to close my eyes, the Mother appeared in front of me. Next day I again returned to the Ashram. After the second meeting with Mother, my desire to break off the fetters of worldliness intensified. Thinking of the Mother, I became like one who is crazy. I forgot to eat, sleep and to take bath. I left off my fancy way of dressing and combing the hair. My parents and other family members no­ ticed the change in me and becoming worried, forbid me from going to Vallickavu. “The next day after participating in the bhajan, I entered the shrine with this resolve: ‘Mother, if I am your child, please accept me.’ Placing my head on her shoulder. Mother lov­ ingly said, ‘Son, when Mother heard you singing, she under­ stood that this voice is meant to merge in God. Then itself Mother came near you and made you one with her. You are my very own.’ “One night when I
150: was half asleep, I smelt a peculiar fra­ grance pervading the whole room. I opened my eyes and found that the fragrance was really there, not just a dream or imagination. Suddenly, I felt someone’s hands stroking my forehead. I looked up and to my surprise I saw Mother stand­ ing at the end of the cot where my head was. I couldn’t believe248 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI my eyes. She smiled at me and said, ‘My son. Mother is always with you, don't worry.' Saying this, she disappeared. Next morning I rushed to Vallickavu hut Mother was not there. She came only at 4 o'clock in the evening. Without ut­ tering a word, she ran to the house and came out with a plate of rice and fed me like a mother would her son. While feeding me she said, ‘Last night. Mother came near you? Overwhelmed with happiness, I cried like a small child. In fact, I had not eaten anything that day. “Having been initiated by Mother with a mantra, I was unable to stay at home. My yearning to live in her presence and to seek her instructions increased day by day. Disregarding the obstacles created by my relatives, I left my home and joined the Ashram. “Two years later, whilst sitting in a devotee’s house she said to me, ‘My son Balu, you must get a Master’s degree in philosophy.' I had previously told Mother one day that I was not going to continue my studies but just wanted to become crazy thinking about her. Now, two years later she was asking me to study again. From my experience I knew that without a purpose she would not say or do anything and so I joined the course. But now arose a real problem; who would teach me? I would have to study eight papers, four on Indian philosophy with which I was somewhat familiar and four on Western phi­ losophy which was an entirely new subject. The need of an instructor was imperative. I asked Mother about it. ‘Don’t worry about it. Someone will come here and in­ struct you. Patiently wait and see' she told. But I was restless and frequently disturbed her asking the same question. After one week, a devotee directed me to a man who was a philoso­ phy professor. I went to his place and explained the situation He was ready to teach me but refused to come to the Ashram. I tried to make him understand about my difficulty in leaving the Ashram to study. At last he agreed to visit the Ashram but said, ‘I cannot stay there or give classes for you there. If you want to study you must come here. Otherwise, drop the mat­ ter now itself.' I thought that since I had no other choice let him atleast come to the Ashram and see Mother.Experiences Of Devotees 249 “The following Thursday I went and fetched him from his home. When we reached the Ashram, I requested
151: him to come and see Mother but he refused. When Mother sat for her usual singing before the Bhava Darshan, he was watching from a distance. Even after the Darshan started he was closely ob­ serving from a distance. I approached him and told him that if he liked he could go inside the temple and have Mother’s Darshan. ‘No, I have never prostrated to anyone before. I don’t like to do that,’ he replied. I left him alone and sat for singing. After a couple of minutes I saw him rushing into the temple and heard a loud cry. He had fallen flat in front of Mother and was weeping like a small child. An hour or two passed. Coming out of the shnne, he called me aside and said, €Shc is a Great Soul par excellence. Every week I will come here and teach you.’ Thus, Mother herself arranged a teacher for me. “Referring to different books, the professor dictated a lot of notes to me but didn't explain anything. Unfortunately, due to certain other reasons we were not able to continue the studies regularly and Western philosophy still remained as an unknown subject to me. Only three more months were left before the examinations. The professor dictated to me some more notes and gave a summary’ of the whole thing. As I was involved in different activities of the Ashram and would fre­ quently be travelling with Mother, I could not attend to my studies. Now there was only one month left for the exams. Mother asked me to write all the eight papers in one sirring. I was really worried as to how I would be able to write both the first and final year papers together. I dedicated everything at Mother's feet and began reading. At last it was the day before my departure to Tirupati (a town in Andhra Pradesh some 700 miles from the Ashram) where I had registered my name in the University as a student of philosophy. “At noon I was about to pack my things. Suddenly, I heard Mother calling me from her room. I ran to her room and saw her packing some things in a bag. She put the last item into it and dosed it. Another big bag was there on her table. With 250 MATA AMRITANANDAMAYI great affection she came near me and said, “Son, I have packed everything for your journey.” Pointing to the bag placed on the table she said, “That bag contains dhotis, shirts, towels, two blankets and other clothes and in this one there is coconut oil, soap, a mirror, comb, something to make a hot drink and other useful things. I have packed all these things so as to save time for your studies.” I was unable to utter even a word. I simply looked at her loving face. My heart was overflowing with joy. My eyes filled with tears and I broke down weeping. “After coming to her, this was the
